job.title: Inventory Controller
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Inventory Controller located in Broken Arrow, OK. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and has been recognized as one of the most ethical companies in the world

  • Comprehensive benefits package starting Day One of employment – medical, dental, vision, 401k, tuition reimbursement and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Receiving function for incoming inventory

  • Inventory management for individual parts and finished goods inventory

  • Inventory transactions in Oracle

  • Cycle counts to maintain inventory accuracy

  • Staging of parts for the assembly operation

  • Management and coordination of orders to local machine shops for critical inventory needs

  • Material transfers, both physically and in the system

  • Arrangements for outgoing shipments to the field operations teams

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • High School education or equivalent

  • 2 years relevant experience

  • Ability to drive a forklift

  • Attention to detail

  • Interact politely and professionally with customers and/or coworkers

  • Effectively present information and respond to questions

  • Intermediate verbal and written communication skills

  • Basic computer skills, including Microsoft Office Suite

  • Able to complete required documentation

  • Follow all safety policies and company-wide safety requirements; encourage action assuring safe behavior; confront unsafe behavior and conditions proactively and positively

  • Regularly and predictably attend work and be punctual

  • Work in excess of 8 hours per day and 40 hours per week, as required to meet business needs

  • Read, write, and speak English fluently

Physical Demands:

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.  Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • Medium work:  Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects.

  • Climbing:  Ascending or descending ladders, stairs, scaffolding, ramps, poles and the like, using feet and legs and/or hands and arms. Body agility is emphasized.

  • Balancing:  Maintaining body equilibrium to prevent falling when walking, standing, or crouching on narrow, slippery, or erratically moving surfaces.

  • Stooping:  Bending body downward and forward by bending spine at the waist. This factor is important if it occurs to a considerable degree and requires full use of the lower extremities and back muscles.

  • Kneeling:  Bending legs at knee to come to a rest on knees.

  • Crouching:  Bending the body downward and forward by bending leg and spine.

  • Crawling:  Moving about on hands and knees or hands and feet.

  • Reaching:  Extending hand(s) and arm(s) in any direction.

  • Walking:  Moving about on foot to accomplish tasks, particularly for long distances or moving from one work site to another.

  • Pushing:  Using upper extremities to press against something with steady force in order to thrust forward, downward, or outward.

  • Pulling:  Using upper extremities to exert force in order to draw, drag, haul, or tug objects in a sustained motion.

  • Lifting:  Raising objects from a lower to a higher position or moving objects horizontally from position to position.

  • Fingering:  Picking, pinching, typing, or otherwise working, primarily with fingers rather than the whole hand or arm as in handling.

  • Grasping:  Applying pressure to an object with the fingers and palm.

  • Feeling:  Perceiving attributes of objects, such as size, shape, temperature, or texture by touching with skin, particularly that of fingertips.

  • Talking:  Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word.  Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to customer or other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly.

  • Hearing:  Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction.  Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound.

  • Repetitive motion:  Substantial movement (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers to use a computer.

  • The employee is required to have close visual acuity to perform an activity such as: visual inspection involving small defects, small parts and/or operation of machines (including inspection), using measurement devices, and/or assembly or fabrication of parts at distances close to the eyes.

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to operate motor vehicles.

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to determine the accuracy, neatness, thoroughness of work assigned or to make general observations of facilities or structures.

job.title: Electronics Technician II
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Electronics Technician II for our Repairs department at Windrock located in Knoxville, TN. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • The Electronics Technician will provide support activities for engineering, manufacturing, and repair.

  • Trouble-shoot various types of electronic equipment to component level.

  • Perform a variety of repairs from component replacement to extensive modification of analyzer equipment.

  • Assist in testing new products.

  • Maintain internal tracking databases.

  • Provide engineering, manufacturing, and repair personnel with information and feedback.

  • Assist with development of test procedures and equipment.

  • Provide internal feedback on common quality issues.

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • Associate degree in electrical/Electronic Engineering or a similar field

  • 5 years of experience in a similar role.

  • Proficient in use of equipment such as: DMM, Function Generator, Oscilloscope

  • Must be able to interpret schematics.

  • Strong Mechanical Aptitude.

  • Detail Oriented.

  • Fully computer literate such as:  Windows OS, Microsoft Office Suite, WinZip.

  • Operate in a Team Environment.

  • Technical Communication (Written/Oral).

  • Good personal presence/relationship skills with customers.

  • Eagerness/Willingness to learn.

  • Ability to handle loads weighing up to 50 pounds.

  • Immigration sponsorship will not be offered for this role.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Any relevant experience in the following areas: Electronic assembly/repair, Solder Inspect/Repair, Customer Service, Knowledge of engines, and other types of reciprocating machinery

Physical Demands:

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands and fingers, to pick, feel and/or inspect material. Employee would also reach with hands and arms as needed.

  • Some work is accomplished indoors and in air conditioned or well-ventilated facilities.

  • Work normally is executed in an office or in a cubicle space equipped with a telephone and computer.

  • Role is deemed safety sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

job.title: Training Specialist I
job.description:
Job Description

Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has immediate and future hiring needs for a Training Specialist I to support site operations by enhancing workforce capability, communication, and compliance. This is an exciting opportunity to join a growing organization that values innovation, safety, and continuous development, while offering a competitive compensation package and comprehensive benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, technology, and continuous learning opportunities
  • Thrive in a company that prioritizes safety, including industry-leading training and personal protective equipment
  • Pride in working for an organization that supports clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments
  • Opportunities to contribute to employee development, engagement, and operational excellence
  • Comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, and 401(k) with company match

What You Will Do:

Training Delivery

  • Facilitate engaging, interactive training sessions using modern techniques such as simulations, role play, video, and digital tools
  • Lead training sessions confidently while partnering with Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) for technical accuracy
  • Present on camera for training videos and microlearning content
  • Select appropriate delivery methods (in-person, virtual, blended) based on training needs

Instructional Design & Content Creation

  • Develop training materials, videos, scripts, assessments, and job aids using multimedia and AI-supported tools
  • Apply adult learning principles, including Bloom’s Taxonomy and multimodal learning strategies
  • Translate complex technical topics into clear, engaging, and easy-to-understand content
  • Validate all training materials with SMEs to ensure accuracy and effectiveness

Competency, Retention & Compliance

  • Design and execute retention-focused learning strategies to improve workforce capability
  • Track and report training KPIs, including completion rates, assessment performance, and competency levels
  • Define and measure employee proficiency levels (qualified, experienced, expert)
  • Maintain accurate training records, dashboards, and audit-ready documentation

Communication & Collaboration

  • Serve as a liaison between employees, SMEs, supervisors, and leadership
  • Gather feedback and ensure timely follow-up with appropriate stakeholders
  • Support leadership with clear, concise communication, updates, and summaries
  • Improve site-wide communication clarity by simplifying technical information
  • Participate in plant meetings, Management of Change (MOC) processes, and project discussions

Innovation & Technology

  • Leverage AI tools for note-taking, content creation, and training development
  • Produce multimedia training using video editing tools and in-house production setups
  • Introduce innovative alternatives to traditional training methods
  • Utilize advanced technologies such as simulations, drones, and immersive learning tools

Position Details:

  • Work Environment: On-site, supporting plant operations
  • Ability to support various shifts and training schedules as needed
  • Occasional travel may be required

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Communication, Psychology, Education/Adult Learning, Human Resources, or related field
  • 3 years of experience in training, facilitation, communications, education, or organizational learning
  • Strong presentation and public speaking skills, including comfort presenting to live audiences and on camera
  • Experience developing training materials and applying adult learning principles
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Strong organizational skills and attention to detail

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience in industrial, manufacturing, energy, logistics, or chemical environments
  • Background in instructional design, adult learning, organizational psychology, or communication studies
  • Familiarity with OSHA/SHE compliance training
  • Experience with Learning Management Systems (LMS) or training databases
  • Proficiency with technology, AI tools, video editing, and multimedia production
  • Strong time management, adaptability, and problem-solving skills

Physical Demands:

  • May be required to move throughout a plant or operational environment
  • Occasional lifting, standing, and walking may be required
  • Role may be subject to safety requirements and site-specific protocols

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

job.title: Production Team Leader
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has immediate and future hiring needs for a Production Team Leader in Freeport, TX to support site operations. This is an opportunity to join a growing organization focused on operational excellence, safety, and continuous improvement, offering a competitive compensation package and comprehensive benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, technology, and continuous education
  • Thrive in a company that values a strong safety culture, including top-tier safety training and personal protective equipment
  • Pride in working for a company that supports clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments
  • Opportunity to lead teams and directly impact operational performance and continuous improvement
  • Comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, and 401(k) with company matching and more

What You Will Do:

  • Lead, train, and mentor a team of production associates to drive performance and engagement
  • Oversee day-to-day production activities to ensure safe, efficient, and high-quality operations
  • Provide skill-based instruction, coaching, and feedback while addressing personnel matters at the shift level
  • Ensure compliance with plant rules, policies, and safety standards; reinforce accountability across the team
  • Collaborate with the Technical Team to ensure all batches meet specifications, procedures, and documentation requirements
  • Clearly communicate shift objectives and align team performance with overall plant goals
  • Understand and communicate key performance metrics, ensuring team awareness and accountability
  • Monitor equipment performance, troubleshoot issues, and identify opportunities for efficiency improvements
  • Lead training and development progression for new and current employees
  • Partner with cross-functional teams (Quality, Planning, Engineering, Procurement, Logistics) to resolve production, service, and quality challenges
  • Support development of the site operations budget with a focus on continuous improvement of key performance metrics
  • Drive and promote Continuous Improvement initiatives to enhance safety, quality, delivery, and cost performance
  • Review procedures and documentation to ensure accuracy and adherence to SOPs and Good Documentation Practices
  • Coordinate operational improvement and redesign projects to meet performance targets and customer needs
  • Lead or participate in Root Cause Analysis to resolve safety, quality, and service issues
  • Perform additional duties as assigned

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s Degree OR High School Diploma / GED with 4 years of experience in manufacturing, logistics, or related environment
  • 2 years of supervisory experience in a manufacturing or logistics environment
  • Strong leadership and team development experience
  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Engineering Degree, Process Technology Degree, or equivalent
  • Experience with SAP, MS Access, and Microsoft Excel
  • Exposure to Continuous Improvement and/or Lean Manufacturing principles
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to exercise independent judgment, think critically, and collaborate across cross-functional teams
  • Proven ability to drive accountability, improve processes, and lead change initiatives

Physical Demands:

  • May be required to lift, push, pull, or carry materials as needed in a plant environment
  • Ability to work in a manufacturing setting, including standing, walking, and interacting with equipment
  • Role may be subject to safety requirements and site-specific protocols

job.title: Local HazMat CDL Driver
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We have an immediate need for an Operational Specialist in McCook, Nebraska.  If you are a dedicated DOT driving professional with a proven track record of success in customer delivery, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base hourly rate and benefits. 

What's in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits
  • Access to best in class resources, tools, and technology
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection
  • Comprehensive benefits package (Medical, Dental, Vision, 401K and much more) that starts Day 1
  • $4,000 Hiring Bonus

What You Will Do:

This position will primarily be responsible for providing chemical treatments to oil wells, and the maintenance and safe operation of any type of vehicle used to deliver chemicals to customers, plants and/or warehouse locations.

  • Chemical treating of all scheduled wells and locations on a regular basis
  • Maintain chemical inventory reports
  • Present a professional manner and work closely with salespeople to satisfy customer requirements.
  • Maintain regular communication with sales and supervisor in order to report field problems and/or safety hazards
  • Maintain truck and equipment in a safe and operable condition by performing daily and periodic mechanical and safety inspections
  • Maintain driver's hours of service by using Turnpike and/or paper logbook reporting
  • Keeps current on safety techniques and take advantage of all opportunities to improve driving and safety skills
  • Recognize and avoid safety hazards in the work environment
  • Properly load trucks, prepare daily bills of lading, schedule the order of daily treatments and submit treating reports to sales

Territory / Location Information:

  • This will be a local position that will be based in and will be responsible for chemical treatment and delivery within a 50-mile radius of McCook, NE
  • Candidate must reside within 50 miles of McCook, NE
  • Relief positions also available with a $2.00 an hour travel bonus

Schedule:

  • Multiple Schedules available
  • Monday Thru Friday with an occasional Saturday
  • Weekend rotation availability with days off in the week
  • 10-12 hour day time shifts

Basic/Minimum Qualifications:

  • Minimum high school graduate or GED
  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate company vehicle
  • Must currently hold a valid Class A or B Commercial Driver's License (CDL), issued by the state in which the candidate resides
  • Must have HazMat and Tanker Endorsements or be willing to be obtain within 30 days
  • One (1) year of commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past three years required
  • Acceptable motor vehicle record (3 years)
  • All candidates must have the ability to speak, read and write English
  • Immigration sponsorship is not offered for this role
  • Must be comfortable working with cameras in the truck cab
  • No manual transmission restrictions

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Carry up to 50 pounds chest high.
  • Must be able to pass a drug screen and physical exam

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Three (3) years commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past five years required
  • Experience with the operation of forklifts, drum dollies, trailer hoists, chemical pumps, hoses, meters, printers and gauges
  • Experience with handling products in various types of packages including bulk, drums, Porta-Feeds and mini/micro-feed units
  • Knowledge of precautions needed if exposed to H2S (hydrogen sulfide)
  • Experience in customer relations
  • Experience performing warehouse duties, which include preventative maintenance, inventory and ordering of chemicals, receiving incoming shipments, organizing warehouse for safe and efficient chemical storage, operation of customized chemical manufacturing equipment and the upkeep of warehouse grounds
  • Awareness of chemical safety and the measures to take in the event of skin contact or spill

Position Compensation and Benefits

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!
  • Hourly and overtime pay earning potential between $75,000 to $95,000 a year!

job.title: Reservoir Engineer
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are searching for a Reservoir Engineer for our Technology team located in Sugar Land, Texas.  As a Reservoir Engineer, you will provide reservoir engineering support for assigned Chemically Enhanced Oil Recovery (CEOR) projects by coordinating activities with others within the team to collaboratively ensure targets are met. You will act as a liaison with internal and external customers and provide technical reports and presentations to diverse audiences. Through your work, you will perform and interpret relevant reservoir engineering analysis and draw appropriate conclusions.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Provide reservoir engineering support that includes initial screening, data collection, engineering analysis, numerical simulation/prediction in both lab and field scales for CEOR projects (both R&D and customer-based projects)

  • Manage assigned projects and monitor progress to ensure targets are met, while liaising with internal and external customers.

  • Coordinate activities with the other groups involved (Technology, Marketing, Sales and other reservoir engineers).

  • Split your time between technical support projects and customer interaction.  It is expected that this position will spend minimum of 25% in customer-facing meetings.

  • Writing/presenting technical reports/publications (internally & externally)

  • Some advanced data analytics

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree in Petroleum, Chemical  or Mechanical Engineering

  • 6 years of relevant work experience in IOR (Improved Oil Recovery) / EOR (Enhanced Oil Recovery) techniques

  • Prior IOR/EOR experience in academic or industry setting

  • Understating of multi-phase flow through porous media, fluids property modelling  (phase behavior), routine and special core analysis

  • Experience in reservoir engineering including (not limited to) reservoir management, waterflooding performance evaluation/optimization, well testing,  interwell tracer data analysis 

  • Solid knowledge and experience in reservoir simulation including model construction, history matching, calibration and prediction

  • Proficient in Eclipse, CMG reservoir simulation software

  • Must be a creative thinker with the ability to build conceptual models to implement new ideas, processes and technologies

  • Proven ability to interact with a variety of contacts, both internal and external, including management and industry partners to identify and implement advanced reservoir engineering technologies.

  • Self-driven and ability to work under minimal supervision 

Preferred Qualifications:

  • PhD in Petroleum, Chemical  or Mechanical Engineering with 2 years of relevant work experience or MS with 4 years of relevant work experience

  • Advanced data analytics experience

  • Demonstrated organizational skills

  • Detail-orientated with excellent time management skills

  • Excellent oral and written communication skills

  • Demonstrated interpersonal skills to work effectively in a team environment and maintain a professional and positive attitude

  • Demonstrated ability to work and think independently

  • Project leadership/management experience

  • Open to new ideas and innovations

  • Ability to analyze and interpret complex data

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

  • Must be able to lift up to 30 pounds

job.title: Senior Mechanical Technician
job.description:
ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.


We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

For more news and information, visit SLB.com

Follow us on Facebook at @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, or Instagram at @SLBGlobal.

We are looking for a Senior Mechanical Technician located in Singapore. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

Job Overview:

The Senior Mechanical Technician is responsible for ensuring the reliability, safety, and performance of mechanical equipment in the plant. The role focuses on preventive and corrective maintenance, troubleshooting, and condition monitoring of rotating and static equipment to minimize downtime and support plant operations.

What You will do:

  • Installation, calibration, inspection, and troubleshooting of mechanical equipment, in accordance with Engineering and Company Standards.

  • Carry out preventive and breakdown maintenance efficiently and effectively for process and utilities equipment and building facilities.

  • Assist in coordinating and ensuring the quality of work carried out by external and internal contractors are as per requirement.

  • Maintain and service rotating equipment such as reactors, pumps, agitators, air compressors and ancillaries.

  • Maintain and service static equipment such as heat-exchangers, vessels, tanks and ancillaries.

  • Perform shaft laser alignment and use vibration analysis tool to collect data.

  • Maintain the mechanical spare parts inventory.

  • Develop written job plans for mechanical projects.

  • Develop written procedures for mechanical equipment maintenance and inspection.

  • Maintain the mechanical spare parts inventory.

  • Plan and direct procurement of repair parts and material for mechanical equipment for project and maintenance purposes.

  • Support Plant Operations to minimize production impacts and enhance reliability.

  • Maintain high standards of workmanship and professionalism while working as a member of a team or when working alone.

  • Participate in reliability / TPM programs to improve availability rates for all process mechanical facilities.

  • Maintain proper records of all jobs performed in the SAP and CMMS application and participate in mechanical related projects and plant modification work.

Minimum Qualifications

  • Nitec or Diploma in Mechanical engineering or Equivalent

  • Minimum 10 years of relevant work experience in process or mechanical disciplines is required, with foundational exposure to instrumentation and electrical trades.

Preferred Qualifications

  • ​Prior experience working in chemical or process plant environments is an advantage.

  • Willingness to work overtime when operational requirements demand.

  • Available to participate in an on‑call rotation to support 24/7/365 facility operations.

  • Must be able to respond to call‑outs within 30 to 60 minutes of notification.

  • Preferred to be able to use and navigate business applications including Computerized Maintenance Management Systems (CMMS) in SAP and proficiency in Microsoft Office applications is preferred.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. job.title: District Representative III
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has an immediate need for a District Representative located in Midland, TX. If you are a passionate sales professional with a proven track record of success in technical sales we invite you to apply. The District Representative is responsible for overseeing client relationships and commercial activities related to the midstream, gas treating, and gas processing. You will be primarily responsible for revenue and profit growth of ChampionX programs and services in targeted accounts within the Oil and Gas industries.  You will be required to take a consultative sales approach with a primary emphasis on strong account leadership skills and retention of strategic ChampionX accounts. In addition, you must build long-term relationships with a large customer base by understanding their key business drivers, executing system assurance programs, and selling new technologies.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!
  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Act as the primary contact for clients involved in refined product sales, transportation, and logistics.
  • Collaborate with supply chain, logistics, and operations teams to ensure timely and accurate product delivery.
  • Monitor market trends, regulatory changes, and customer feedback to identify opportunities for improvement and growth.
  • Develop and maintain strong client relationships to support retention and expansion.
  • Prepare and present performance reports, forecasts, and proposals to clients and internal stakeholders.
  • Resolve service issues and coordinate with internal teams to ensure customer satisfaction.
  • Support business development initiatives and contribute to strategic planning.

Position Details:

  • Work week and shift:
  • Up to 10% overnight travel required to support sales territory
  • Ability to travel within assigned sales territory

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business or Engineering.
  • Minimum of 3 years of experience in water utility treatment experience.
  • Strong understanding of product specifications, blending, and logistics.
  • Excellent communication, negotiation, and relationship management skills.

job.title: Account Manager
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Account Manager in Meota, SK. 

You will be a self starter, that has excellent time management skills and a desire to grow your accounts. In addition, you will be responsible for building long-term relationships throughout your customer base by understanding their key business drivers, executing system assurance programs, and selling new technologies.

If you are a passionate sales professional with a proven track record of success in technical sales, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base salary, bonus structure and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • A flexible and independent work environment that fosters a good work-life balance.

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement.

  • Employee Resource Groups for employees.

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes extended health benefits, a company-paid pension plan, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Work closely with current and new customers within an assigned territory/customer base.  Understand customer business needs and recommend continuous improvement and innovation plans that will maintain and grow sales.

  • Develop strong relationships with key stakeholders in current and new customer organizations, including plant or facility executives.

  • Generate and execute sales plans in assigned customer base and new customer accounts to meet defined territory profit increase goals.

  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer issues, escalating as required.

  • Engage in problem solving by performing system analysis, interpreting data and providing written recommendations to ensure customer operations are performing at optimal levels.

  • Actively sell and support ChampionX innovations and technology in assigned customers to promote long-term business relationships with ChampionX.

  • Possible travel within assigned sales territory

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in Meota, SK or nearby area.

    • Candidate is expected to reside within a reasonable distance from the business they support in Meota.

  • No immigration sponsorship offered.

  • This is a safety-sensitive position, and the successful candidate will be required to complete drug and alcohol testing.

  • This role requires individual to be clean shaven to comply with customer site policy and safety requirement

  • Must have a valid Driver’s License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 3 -5 years of technical sales or field sales support experience is preferred but not required.

  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite, and ability to learn internal business systems)

  • Prior experience that demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multitask

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience in the oil and gas industry preferred

  • Previous Petrochemical experience

  • Water treatment or specialty chemical industry experience

  • Working knowledge of water treatment systems

  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation

  • Prior experience that required excellent communication skills

  • Prior experience that required excellent organizational skills

  • Account leadership experience that includes calling on multilevel plant or facilities management to accomplish company goals

  • Must be able to closely work with and support a very dynamic team of Account Managers.

job.title: Electronics Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration. We have an immediate need for an Electronics Technician. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture, and empowers through continuous improvement

What You Will Do:

  • Uses mastery of electrician trades skills and knowledge to diagnose and solve electrical problems in manufacturing equipment, machines, and equipment.

  • Creates and assembles components and installs them as required.

  • Performs preventative maintenance.

  • Document maintenance and repairs as required.

  • Interacts fluently with those in other trades and work areas to complete work assignments; provides technical electrical expertise to mechanics, facilities, and other work teams.

  • Facilitates repair of electrical and electronic equipment, induction heating.

  • Design electrical and electronic equipment.

  • Edit program changes in automated machines using PLC logic.

  • Maintain parts and materials inventory for electronic equipment.

  • Formulates maintenance of computer programming.

  • Calibrating eddy current inspection equipment and building coils for same.

  • Ensure proper heat control on heat-treating furnaces.

  • Check calibration of measuring devices on RMC.

  • Contributes to the department continuous improvement culture.

  • Knows and complies with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Maintains a clean and well-organized work area.

  • Maintains confidentiality of all matters.

  • Other duties as required.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma, GED or equivalent required with Department of Labor Certification OR Associates degree from an accredited program in Electrical/Electronic Engineering, or a related field or equivalent education and/or experience.

  • 2 years of experience in a similar manufacturing role

  • Good oral and written communication skills, including the ability to follow written and oral instructions.

  • Good math and analytical skills.

  • Good organizational and time management skills.

  • Good computer skills, including Microsoft Office Suite.

Physical Demands:  

  • Must be able to lift and carry 50 lbs regularly.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • The employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

  • The employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

  • Permanent

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • A college or university diploma or degree in Business, Supply Chain Management, Purchasing, Logistics or related field

  • Excellent math and analytical skills

job.title: Permian Sales Lead - Emissions
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Permian Sales Lead located in Midland, Texas. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

The Permian Sales Lead is responsible for driving revenue growth, supporting strategic sales initiatives, and leading day-to-day customer engagement activities. This role partners closely with cross-functional teams such as Marketing, Operations, Product Development and Customer Success to ensure alignment of customer needs, market opportunities, and commercial priorities. The Permian Sales Lead serves as a key point of contact for clients while guiding internal teams to deliver solutions that support both short- and long-term business objectives.

What's In It For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement.

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Revenue Growth & Pipeline Management:

  • Develop, manage, and grow a robust sales pipeline within assigned markets and accounts.

  • Identify new business opportunities and lead prospecting efforts.

  • Support forecasting, reporting, and performance metrics to inform business decisions.

  • Client Relationship Management

  • Serve as a primary contact for customers, ensuring a high level of satisfaction and proactive communication.

  • Conduct regular client meetings to understand business needs and present tailored solutions.

  • Build long-term partnerships by delivering a consultative sales approach.

  • Sales Strategy & Execution:

  • Partner with leadership to execute sales strategies aligned with company objectives.

  • Analyze market trends, competition, and customer insights to guide commercial decisions.

  • Support pricing strategies and proposal development.

  • Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Work closely with Operations, Product, Marketing, and Finance teams to ensure seamless delivery of customer commitments.

  • Provide customer feedback that informs product development and service enhancements.

  • Leadership & Coaching:

  • Mentor junior sales team members and support onboarding of new hires.

  • Promote a culture of accountability, performance, and continuous improvement.

  • Other duties as required.

  • Maintains confidentiality of all matters.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Engineering, Atmospheric Sciences, Business or related field, minimum of five years’ experience in Oil & Gas segment with strategic connections with Operators in Permian area.

  • 5 years of experience in sales, business development, or account management; industry experience may be required depending on the role.

  • Proven track record of achieving or exceeding sales targets.

  • Strong communication, negotiation, and presentation skills.

  • Ability to build trusted relationships with both internal and external stakeholders.

  • Proficiency with CRM systems, sales analytics tools, and Microsoft Office Suite.

  • Ability to multitask.

  • Strong attention to detail.

  • Ability to work without direct supervision.

  • Collaborative attitude and strong work ethic with the ability to enthusiastically represent the company.

  • 60-80% availability for inter-regional travel.

  • Service oriented attitude.

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • 5 years in a similar sales position.

  • Experience and Knowledge of ESG, Emissions and/or Atmospheric Sciences

  • Oil & gas industry experience.

  • History of working in a field that requires autonomy and self-motivation.

  • Prior experience demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multi-task.

  • Prior experience that required excellent organizational skills.

isclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has immediate and future needs for a Chemical Service Technician located in Baker, MT. Works with inventory in a warehouse environment under direct supervision.

job.title: Process Engineer
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has an immediate need for a Process Engineer II located in Odessa, TX. This position will work both in the office and in the plant, reporting to the Engineering and Maintenance Manager.

This role provides technical support to Safety, Operations, Logistics, Quality, and Maintenance teams, while leading plant process improvements, supporting capital projects, and assisting with new product implementation.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth
  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best
  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability and drives a safety-focused culture
  • Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Provide daily technical support for the assigned production unit.
  • Analyze, troubleshoot, and resolve issues that arise during daily production operations.
  • Enhance process efficiency and production capacity while maintaining or improving quality standards and lowering operating costs.
  • Manage New Product Set-Ups and Scale Ups from R&D and Marketing.
  • Perform and maintain the Management of Change (MOC) Process.
  • Assist with Design Safety Reviews and PHAs where critical thinking is required.
  • Lead and participate in continuous improvement initiatives (CI) to deliver conversion cost targets, unlock throughput, and improve Odessaemployee engagement.
  • Develop and implement small capital projects. This includes the ability to manage a project from conception through construction to startup.
  • Develop and implement plant projects. This includes the ability to do design work such as piping, instrumentation, controls, and equipment specification.
  • Coordinates projects to effect timely, cost-effective, and safe completion within set quality standards.
  • Ensure that all design and project work comply with applicable legislation, accepted engineering codes and standards, and Company policies and standards.
  • Understands Process Safety Management (PSM) procedures to ensure that the site is in compliance.
  • Support the plant safety and environmental/ compliance initiatives.

Minimum Qualifications and Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in chemical or mechanical engineering
  • Three years of experience as a Process Engineer in a Chemical Manufacturing Plant

Preferred Skills, Abilities, Knowledge:

  • Advanced Technical Degree
  • Five (5) years of experience as a Process Engineer in a Batch Chemical Manufacturing Plant
  • Ability to read and understand technical drawings such as PFDs, P&IDs, isometric drawings, electrical diagrams, and loop diagrams.
  • Knowledge and experience of relevant Safety, Health, and Environmental regulations
  • Experience in troubleshooting and RCA methods
  • Experience in Data Analysis and Data visualization (Power BI)
  • Project management and contractor management experience
  • Strong grasp of Management of Change processes
  • Strong organizational and time management skills
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Demonstrated ability to participate in and lead cross-functional teams

job.description:

job.title: Shipping/Receiving Coordinator (Temporary)
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Temporary Shipping & Receiving Coordinator for our Rocky View, AB location.

The Shipping & Receiving Coordinator is responsible for receiving, inspecting, organizing, and shipping materials and fabricated equipment in a manufacturing/fabrication environment. This role requires familiarity with fabrication materials, the ability to review Material Test Reports (MTRs), strong organizational skills, and the ability to perform accurate transactions in SAP.

This is a temporary 1-year contract

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and has been recognized as one of the most ethical companies in the world

What Will You Do:

  • Receive incoming materials and fabricated components, verifying quantities, condition, and documentation against purchase orders (POs).

  • Coordinate outbound shipments, including packaging, labeling, and scheduling with carriers.

  • Prepare standard shipping documentation such as packing lists, bills of lading, and USMCA documentation

  • Maintain organized warehouse, yard, and staging areas to support fabrication and shipment flow.

  • Review and verify Material Test Reports (MTRs) for completeness and alignment with received materials.

  • Confirm material traceability including heat numbers and material grades.

  • Work with QC and fabrication teams to resolve discrepancies related to materials or documentation.

  • Perform goods receipt, inventory movements, and shipment transactions in SAP.

  • Ensure all materials are received against the correct purchase orders and projects.

  • Track expected PO arrivals and communicate issues related to late, early, or incomplete deliveries.

  • Maintain accurate inventory records and support cycle counts as required.

  • Follow all site safety procedures related to material handling, lifting, and forklift operation.

  • Maintain a clean, safe, and orderly shipping and receiving area.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma or equivalent.

  • 3-7 years of experience in shipping and receiving within a fabrication or manufacturing environment. Familiarity with fabrication materials such as steel, pipe, and industrial components.

  • Ability to review and understand Material Test Reports (MTRs).

  • Proficiency with SAP for receipts, inventory, and PO management.

  • Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

  • Comfortable working in a shop environment.

  • Strong and clear communication skills.

  • Takes ownership of documentation and material accuracy.

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in Calgary or Rocky View, AB or the surrounding area.

  • This position is Monday to Friday on-site, based on 40 hours per week.

  • Immigration Sponsorship is not available for this role.

Physical Demands:

  • Ability to push, pull, lift and carry up to 50lbs

  • This role is a safety-sensitive position, and the successful candidate will be required to complete pre-employment testing in accordance with company policy.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Forklift certification or willingness to obtain.

job.title: District Representative
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

SLB/ChampionX has an immediate need for a District Representative located in Hobbs, NM. If you are a passionate sales professional with a proven track record of success in technical sales we invite you to apply. The District Representative is responsible for overseeing client relationships and commercial activities related to the oil and gas production systems. You will be primarily responsible for revenue and profit growth of ChampionX programs and services in targeted accounts within the Oil and Gas industries.  You will be required to take a consultative sales approach with a primary emphasis on strong account leadership skills and retention of strategic ChampionX accounts. In addition, you must build long-term relationships with a large customer base by understanding their key business drivers, executing system assurance programs, and selling new technologies.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!
  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Act as the primary contact for clients involved in refined product sales, transportation, and logistics.
  • Collaborate with supply chain, logistics, and operations teams to ensure timely and accurate product delivery.
  • Monitor market trends, regulatory changes, and customer feedback to identify opportunities for improvement and growth.
  • Develop and maintain strong client relationships to support retention and expansion.
  • Prepare and present performance reports, forecasts, and proposals to clients and internal stakeholders.
  • Resolve service issues and coordinate with internal teams to ensure customer satisfaction.
  • Support business development initiatives and contribute to strategic planning.

Position Details:

  • Work week and shift:
  • Up to 10% overnight travel required to support sales territory
  • Ability to travel within assigned sales territory

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Associates degree in Business, Science, Math
  • Minimum of 3 years of chemical experience.
  • Strong understanding of product specifications, blending, and logistics.
  • Excellent communication, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
  • Proficiency in CRM systems, Microsoft Office Suite, and data analysis tools.
  • Ability to travel to client sites and operational facilities as needed.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business or Engineering or a STEM-related field.
  • Familiarity with logistics and inventory management systems.
  • Experience with SAP systems
  • 1-2 years of successful technical sales or field sales support experience
  • 1-2 years of experience directly related to well service stimulation business
  • Experience in oil and gas industry
  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)
  • Prior experience that required excellent communication and organizational skills
  • Prior experience that demonstrates strong ethics, effective time management, ability to multitask, adaptability, autonomy and self-motivation

job.title: Pump Repair Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX (SLB) has an immediate need for a Pump Repair Technician located in Odessa, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement.

  • The chance to join a learning and problem-solving culture

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

 

What You Will Do:

  • The Pump Repair Technician’s role is to interface with customers, manage repair of parts and pumps and complete various warehouse management responsibilities.

  • Position requirements include strong interpersonal skills, highly self-motivated, ability to work both independently and team-oriented, mechanically inclined.

  • Manage and pull inventory

  • Assemble and disassemble rod pumps

  • Maintain a clean and safe working environment

  • Load and unload trucks of parts and pumps when delivered to the warehouse

  • Answer phones and greet customers

  • Valid driver's license in state of residence required

  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate company vehicle

  • Some computer skills

  • Good verbal and written communication skills

  • Good driving record

  • Basic math and analytical skills

  • Solid technical skills to understand computerized measuring machines and metalworking processes

  • Ability to read and interpret blueprints and manufacturing documents

  • Must be self-motivated and disciplined to work autonomously with minimal supervision

  • Good organizational skills with the ability to effectively handle multiple tasks

 

Physical Demands:  

  • Ability to lift up to 50lbs

  • Role is deemed safety sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms

 

Pre-Employment Requirements:

job.title: Business Development Manager - Emissions
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Business Development Manager located in California. The Business Development Manager role plays a fundamental part in ensuring business growth. The role requires a unique combination of strategic thinking and tactical sales execution in a player-coach role. The successful candidate will be an entrepreneurial self-starter who is effective in creating and closing opportunities with minimal supervision. The candidate will have a proven record spanning from regional key account management, strategic channel development, and demand creation. A significant success factor will be the ability to leverage existing ChampionX channels in the region to achieve required business results.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement.

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Customer Management:

  • Identify customer's needs and sales opportunities to drive growth.

  • Identify and establish business relationships / needs for each target area.

  • Listen, understand, and convey customer requirements.

  • Identify and leverage resources to perform technical / product training for customers at targeted accounts.

  • Identify and communicate customer requirements for new products.

  • Primary area of responsibility will be the Permian.

Strategy & Planning:

  • Identify and develop strategic account plans leveraging another channel partner (internal and external) resources.

  • Drive sales through direct customer relationships, distributors, and through other ChampionX entities.

  • Work closely with the organization to ensure that product strategies and performance are meeting growth and Annual Operating Plan targets.

  • Work closely with Technology development to understand pros and cons of various technical approaches.

Partner and Channel Development:

  • Identify and develop go to market channels for deeper market penetration.

  • Design and deliver effective contracts, collateral and training programs for partners and channels.

  • Execution:

  • Articulate value story, technology, and product positioning to both business and technical users

  • Excellent communication skills that convey conviction and depth in our value proposition.

  • Create incremental demand, opportunities, and close orders.

  • Maintain expert level of product and application knowledge regarding atmospheric sciences.

  • Meet sales goal targets monthly, quarterly, and yearly.

  • Actively manage channel partners effectiveness, prospecting, closing, forecasting, and KPI’s by using a metrics-based approach to report progress and highlight potential roadblocks with proposed mitigation strategies.

  • Maintain accurate records of target accounts, opportunities, quotes, projects, contacts, and correspondence in company approved databases through our Salesforce CRM

  • Other duties as required.

  • Maintains confidentiality of all matters.

Position Details:

  • Compensation: $120-$130k plus commission

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Engineering, Atmospheric Sciences, Business or related field

  • Five years’ experience in Oil & Gas segment with strategic connections with Operators.

  • Ability to multitask.

  • Strong attention to detail.

  • Exceptional verbal, written, and presentation skills.

  • Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team.

  • Practical computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems).

  • Salesforce (or other CRM) Experience and Oracle.

  • Ability to work without direct supervision.

  • Collaborative attitude and strong work ethic with the ability to enthusiastically represent the company.

  • Availability for inter-regional trave 60-80%.

  • Service oriented attitude.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • 4 years on a similar sales position.

  • Experience and Knowledge of ESG, Emissions and/or Atmospheric Sciences

  • Oil & gas industry experience.

  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation.

  • Prior experience that demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multi-task.

  • Prior experience that required excellent organizational skills.

job.title: Field Service Technician Trainee
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Field Service Technician Trainee located in Odessa, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Works under direct supervision to perform basic customer support activities.

  • Assists higher level technicians with on-site installations, check-out, and emergency repairs.

  • Refers more complex problems to higher level technicians or supervisor.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Associates Degree in Electronics, Automation, or related field or related work experience

  • Basic knowledge of industrial safety

  • Basic knowledge of proper use of hand tools

  • Basic knowledge of wiring and cabling

  • Ability to execute strategies and initiatives

  • Analytical and problem-solving ability

  • Intermediate oral and written communication skills

  • Organization and time management skills

  • Basic math skills

  • Ability to follow prescribed company policies, procedures, and protocols for installation, service and equipment maintenance

  • Interact politely and professionally with customers and/or coworkers

  • Ability to effectively present information and respond to questions from managers, clients, and customers

  • Ability to effectively plan and organize

  • Follow all safety policies and company-wide safety requirements; encourage action assuring safe behavior; confront unsafe behavior and conditions proactively and positively

  • Work in excess of 8 hours per day and 40 hours per week, as required to meet business needs

  • Work in an unsupervised environment

  • Read, write, and speak English fluently

  • Use a computer to communicate, create, and access data

  • Determine the priorities, goals, and objectives of work assigned

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience working with ESP’s

  • Experience working with VFD’s and transformers

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. 

  • Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects.

  • Ascending or descending ladders, stairs, scaffolding, ramps, poles, and the like, using feet and legs and/or hands and arms. Body agility is emphasized.

  • Maintaining body equilibrium to prevent falling when walking, standing, or crouching on narrow, slippery, or erratically moving surfaces.

  • Bending body downward and forward by bending spine at the waist. This factor is important if it occurs to a considerable degree and requires full use of the lower extremities and back muscles.

  • Bending legs at knee to come to a rest on knees.

  • Bending the body downward and forward by bending leg and spine.

  • Moving about on hands and knees or hands and feet.

  • Extending hand(s) and arm(s) in any direction.

  • Moving about on foot to accomplish tasks, particularly for long distances or moving from one work site to another.

  • Using upper extremities to press against something with steady force in order to thrust forward, downward, or outward.

  • Using upper extremities to exert force in order to draw, drag, haul, or tug objects in a sustained motion.

  • Raising objects from a lower to a higher position or moving objects horizontally from position to position.

  • Picking, pinching, typing, or otherwise working, primarily with fingers rather than the whole hand or arm as in handling.

  • Applying pressure to an object with the fingers and palm.

  • Perceiving attributes of objects, such as size, shape, temperature, or texture by touching with skin, particularly that of fingertips.

  • Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word.  Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to customer or other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly.

  • Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction.  Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound.

  • Substantial movement (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers to use a computer.

  • The employee is required to have close visual acuity to perform an activity such as: visual inspection involving small defects, small parts and/or operation of machines (including inspection), using measurement devices, and/or assembly or fabrication of parts at distances close to the eyes.

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to operate motor vehicles.

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to determine the accuracy, neatness, thoroughness of work assigned or to make general observations of facilities or structures.

  • Indoor and outdoor environmental conditions.  Activities occur indoors and outdoors.

  • Extreme heat.  Temperatures above 100 degrees for periods of more than one hour.  Consideration should be given to the effect of other environmental conditions, such as wind and humidity.

  • Hazards.  Includes a variety of physical conditions, such as proximity to moving mechanical parts, moving vehicles, electrical current or exposure to chemicals.

  • Atmospheric conditions.  One or more of the following conditions that affect the respiratory system or the skin:  fumes, odors, dust, mists, gases, or poor ventilation.

  • Oils. There is air and/or skin exposure to oils and other cutting fluids.

job.title: Sr Account Manager
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Sr Account Manager located Bonnyville, AB area.

This position independently manages a portion of the district portfolio with significant accountability to gain new business. Adept at bundling products and anticipating customer needs. Harnesses external network to pursue target business opportunities. Effectively negotiates less complex customer deals. Counsels district representatives and coaches moderate-sized customers. Formal advisor on training curriculum for district representatives.

What’s in it For You:

  • A flexible, independent work environment where you will manage your own schedule

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety to include top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, a company-paid pension plan, stock purchase plan, tuition reimbursement and more!

​

What You Will Do:

  • Work closely with current customers to understand business needs and recommend continuous improvement and innovation plans that will maintain and grow sales within assigned territory.

  • Develop strong relationships with key stakeholders in current customers, including plant or facility executives.

  • Generate and execute sales plans in the assigned customer base to meet defined territory profit increase goals.

  • Develop strong relationships with key stakeholders in current and new customers, including plant or facility executives.

  • Provide technical support to customers, identifying and resolving customer requests, escalating as required.

  • Engage in problem solving by performing system analysis, interpreting data and providing written recommendations to ensure customer operations are performing at optimal levels.

  • Actively sell and support ChampionX innovations and technology in assigned customers to promote long-term business relationships with ChampionX.

  • Possible travel within assigned sales territory

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in the Bonnyville, AB area

  • Immigration sponsorship not available for this position

  • Must have a valid Driver’s License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record (Driver's Abstract)

  • This is a safety-sensitive position, and the successful candidate will be required to complete drug and alcohol testing

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 5-10 years of chemical sales or related experience and operating experience (i.e. power engineering, chemical engineering).

  • Previous Petrochemical experience

  • Ability to relate to customers and their needs.

  • Ability to build relationships with external stakeholders.

  • Account leadership experience that includes calling on multilevel plants or facilities
    management to accomplish company goals.

  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite, and ability to learn internal business systems)

  • History of working autonomously in the field and ability to demonstrate self-motivation

  • Prior experience that demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multitask

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

  • Must have the ability to work in extreme weather conditions.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering (Chemical, Mechanical, Industrial), or Life Sciences (Biology, Chemistry, etc.)

  • Prior experience that required excellent communication skills.

  • Prior experience that required excellent organizational skills.

  • Account leadership experience that includes calling on multilevel plant or facilities management to accomplish company goals.

  • Experience working in the oil and gas industry

job.title: Inside Sales Administrator
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Inside Sales Administrator to support the artificial lift business unit located in NW Houston, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet


This role plays a fundamental part in ensuring business growth alignment between the customer and inside sales staff and outside sales teams. The main responsibility of the Inside Sale Administrator is to be the liaison between our end users, channel partners and inside and outside sales teams while building relationships and collaborating with distribution at the purchasing level through email, phone communication and quoting to receive perfect purchase orders.

What You Will Do:

  • Conduct outbound calls and emails to support communication across Sales and Operations teams

  • Support the Sales team by ensuring timely and responsive follow-up

  • Manage customer price lists and maintain accurate, up-to-date sales records

  • Maintain a high level of attention to detail across data entry, order processing, and reporting

  • Monitor open order reports and take proactive action on items at risk of missing promised delivery dates

  • Process purchase orders and update inventory records in Oracle

  • Prepare and process quotes in a timely manner, ensuring accuracy and proper pricing approvals

  • Act as a liaison between departments to promote effective communication and workflow

  • Manage day-to-day account activities and ensure accurate customer data within internal system

  • Assist with inventory, billing, and operational issue resolution

  • Identify opportunities for continuous improvement in processes and workflows

  • Support our safety-first culture by proactively identifying and reporting safety observations on a regular basis

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Associates degree and 2 years of relevant experience.

  • 2 years work experience in sales administration or similar role.

  • 2 years’ experience in CRM software, Oracle and Microsoft Office (MS Excel particularly).

  • Strong knowledge of sales performance metrics and KPIs.

  • Outstanding organizational and multitasking skills.

  • Team-player mindset with high level of dedication to raising the bar for the whole team.

  • Ability to prioritize work and succeed under deadlines.

  • Ability to effectively communicate with all levels of employees and customers
