job.title: Chemical Data Scientist - Material Informatics
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a highly motivated Chemical Data Scientist in our Chemical Artificial Intelligence group located in Sugar Land, Texas. The Chemical Data Scientist will focus on material informatics and sourcing databases.

 If you are a passionate professional that likes to work in an autonomous, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and has been recognized as one of the most ethical companies in the world.

  • Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Collect vendor/material data (structures, composition, specs, properties, SDS/TDS where available) and maintain data quality/governance.

  • Design canonical schema and mapping logic; implement feature alignment, unit normalization, synonym/entity resolution, and version control.

  • Build analytics-ready datasets for AI/ML or simulation workflows (features, embeddings, metadata, similarity/search).

  • Establish “candidate-to-action” roadmap: supplier options, substitutes, risk flags, and early cost/lead-time signals for buy vs. synthesize decisions.

  • Collaborate with technical team and procurement to validate recommendations and continuously improve database coverage and accuracy.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Ph.D. in Chemistry, Geology, Chemical Engineering, Data Engineering, Petroleum Engineering or related fields with 6+ years of experience; or M.S. in Chemistry, Geology, Chemical Engineering, Petroleum Engineering, or related fields with 8+ years of experience

  • Prior experience with material informatics and sourcing databases

  • Strong data curation + data engineering skills (Python, SQL) and QA automation.

  • Experience standardizing messy multi-source technical datasets (taxonomy, metadata, entity resolution).

  • Feature engineering and alignment across heterogeneous sources; similarity search/cheminformatics is a plus.

  • Ability to work across R&D and sourcing/procurement stakeholders.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Specialty chemicals/materials domain familiarity (surfactants, inhibitors, polymers, solvents, etc.).

  • Experience at a major specialty-chemicals vendor or in supply chain/procurement (vendor management, sourcing strategy, supplier qualification, lead-time/cost drivers).

  • Synthetic chemistry/manufacturing awareness to add feasibility/scale-up and rough cost/manufacturability signals.

job.title: Chemical Data Scientist
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a highly motivated Chemical Data Scientist in our Chemical Artificial Intelligence group located in Sugar Land, Texas. The Chemical Data Scientist will focus on computational chemistry and molecular modelling.

 If you are a passionate professional that likes to work in an autonomous, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and has been recognized as one of the most ethical companies in the world.

  • Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Run and automate simulation workflows to screen candidates and generate property/performance predictions.

  • Apply computational chemistry methods (DFT/MD/Monte Carlo) and interpret outputs to guide R&D decisions.

  • Develop QSAR/QSPR models and analytics linking molecular features to target performance.

  • Select/parameterize force fields; perform QM calculations for key molecular properties.

  • Package simulation/AI results into actionable recommendations and validation plans with lab teams.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Ph.D. in Chemistry, Geology, Chemical Engineering, Petroleum Engineering or related fields with 6+ years of experience; or M.S. in Chemistry, Geology, Chemical Engineering, Petroleum Engineering, or related fields with 8+ years of experience

  • Prior experience in data integration and data engineering

  • Proficiency with molecular simulation software (examples like Biovia Materials Studio).

  • Strong foundation in computational chemistry: DFT, MD, Monte Carlo.

  • Force field selection/parameterization; QM calculations for molecular properties.

  • QSAR/QSPR modeling; data analysis and visualization.

  • Scripting/workflow automation (Python, Perl, or Materials Studio scripting).

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Applied experience in surfactants, corrosion inhibitors, scale inhibitors, polymers, interfacial chemistry.

  • Oil & gas / specialty chemicals industry background.

  • Publication record or strong applied modeling project portfolio.

  • Bonus: synthetic chemistry awareness for feasibility/scale-up and rough manufacturability signals.

job.title: Field Service Technician - HPS
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Field Service Technician to support the Horizontal Pump Systems (HPS) for our Artificial Lift business segment located in Evans, CO. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

A Field Service Technician for the Horizontal Pump Systems is responsible for the installation, maintenance, troubleshooting, and repair of horizontal pump systems (HPS) used in industrial, water treatment, oil & gas, and other process environments. This role requires both mechanical and electrical skills to ensure pumps operate efficiently, safely, and in compliance with industry standards.

What You Will Do:

  • Ensure job deliverables are accurate and delivered on time.

  • Responsible for service delivery execution including installation of horizontal pumps, troubleshooting and perform service inspection.

  • Perform system testing, test pumps for proper flow, pressure and efficiency.

  • Participate in job risk analysis and continual improvement programs.

  • Develop customer contacts to promote company products and services.

  • Ensure company and regulatory standard compliance by implementing the CAT, self-assessments, and well-site inspections.

  • Participate in Service Quality Meetings with customers and improve performance using action plans.

  • Participate in HSE and Quality initiatives.

  • Wear PPE and observe HSE policies.

  • Maintain the required safety training.

Minimum Qualification:

  • Technical certification or high school diploma/GED

  • 2 years’ experience in field service work

  • 2 years' experience with AC/DC motors, SCADA controls and communications systems and/or electrical work.

  • Must have a valid Drivers License

Competency skills

  • Can perform moderately complex pulls and installs independently

  • Strong customer relations skills including the ability to explain and train

  • Knowledge of wiring and cabling

  • Knowledge of proper use of hand tools

  • Strong mechanical and electrical aptitude

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving ability

  • Ability to follow prescribed company policies, procedures, and protocols for installation, service and equipment maintenance

Work Environment

  • Work a minimum of 8 hours per day, and available to work overtime as required to meet business needs.

  • Works both in the shop and on-site at customer locations.

  • May be required to drive for long periods of time

  • Operates in industrial or process environments, often outdoors or in confined spaces.

  • Requires ability to work under pressure, including emergency on-call situations 

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects.

job.title: CNC Maintenance & Facilities Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a CNC Maintenance & Facilities Technician located in Houston, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s In It For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

  • The chance to Improve Lives in our community with regular outreach and volunteerism opportunities

  • The chance to join a learning and problem-solving culture

  • Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, company paid life and disability insurance, health savings account with company provided funds, paid vacation & personal time and so much more!

What You Will Do:

  • Install and repair facility equipment, testing and other production machinery

  • Maintain the maintenance program for CNC Machinery, identify problems and provide repair solutions

  • Troubleshoot facility and production issues due to maintenance or repair required

  • Adhere to the Preventative Maintenance program

  • Coordinate the maintenance of the building/facility including building repairs

  • Coordinate maintenance activities with operations to maximize productivity and minimize downtime of critical equipment

  • Ensure maintenance on production equipment and facility is performed safely, effectively, and expeditiously

  • Perform weekly, monthly, and annual preventative maintenance tasks on various equipment throughout the production areas of the plant

  • Maintain accurate records of maintenance work performed

  • Complete a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for most irregular tasks (Safety is #1)

  • Maintain accurate inventory records, provide regular status reports to Director of Operations

  • Conduct general cleanup to maintain the work area in an orderly condition

  • Interact in a professional manner with employees, vendors, suppliers, and contractors

  • Enforce the Lock Out Tag Out program and provide training when needed

  • Provide technical support to continuously improve process

  • Follow the ISO 9001 Maintenance compliance procedures and documentation

  • Interface closely with other departments to ensure projects involving facility maintenance are defined accurately and executed effectively

  • Assist with facilities and janitorial activities

  • Participate in Lean events with active involvement in CI initiatives

  • Report unsafe acts or conditions to supervision immediately as noticed

Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Satisfactory MVR

  • Drug & Alcohol testing

  • Criminal Background check

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in: Houston TX, or surrounding areas

  • Work week and shift: 5AM-1:30PM, daily some daily overtime and weekend work may be required

  • Compensation package offered: Competitive hourly rate and quarterly bonus eligibility

  • New hire orientation is provided

  • On the job training on processes, procedures, and product assembly

  • Immigration sponsorship is not provided for this position

  • Climate controlled warehouse, standing workstation, strenuous work often required

  • Uniforms provided by company upon regular employment

  • PPE requirements include safety glasses and steel toes

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 5 years of experience in industrial/manufacturing (CNC) machinery maintenance and repair

  • Experience identifying problems and/or providing coordination of repairs for CNC Lathes, Manual Lathes, Mills, production testing and welding equipment

  • Experience in machine preventative and predictive maintenance

  • Experience with electrical and pneumatic equipment

  • Ability to have a flexible schedule and work varying hours, including outside of production activities

  • Must be able to work outdoors occasionally all year round

  • Must be able to read, write and speak English

  • Understand and follow verbal and written instruction

  • Must have a professional and positive attitude as well as a passion for continuous improvement

  • Experience with Microsoft Office Suite

  • Good planning and priority setting skills

  • Strong communication skills, verbal and written

  • Strong organizational and documentation skills

  • Is open to change and new information

  • Show respect, understanding, and concern for others

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • Minimal crawling, balancing, collating/filing and driving motorized equipment/vehicles up to 10%

  • Occasional bending, crouching, kneeling, squatting, climbing ladders, climbing stairs, lifting up to 40lbs, pushing, pulling, fingering, picking, pinching, fine manipulation, using a keyboard and mouse, viewing a computer monitor, dialing, sitting and writing between 11% and 33%

  • Frequent lifting between 10lbs up to 25lbs, reaching, twisting, and stretching between 33% up to 66%

  • Constant handling, holding, grasping, turning, working with hands, standing, walking, seeing, hearing, and speaking greater than 66%

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience with hydraulic equipment

  • Knowledge of Lean Manufacturing concepts

  • Familiarity with ISO 9001 experience for quality standards

job.title: Principal Chemist
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Principal Chemist for our New Product Development (NPD) – New Chemistry Team in Sugar Land, TX. The Principal Chemist will support strategic and transformational innovation projects for Production Chemical Technologies.

The ideal candidate is highly versatile, communicates effectively, and thrives in a collaborative environment.

If you are a passionate professional that likes to work in an autonomous, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, and drives a safety-focused culture

  • Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Adopt, maintain and promote our safety standards, while identifying opportunities for improvement

  • Conduct theoretical or applied scientific research aimed at advancing technical knowledge and/or solving complex problems related to production, reservoir and/or midstream

  • Lead and execute strategic and transformation innovation projects with possible global reach and high impact on the business.

  • Develop novel chemistries/formulations to meet customer needs

  • Develop new testing and/or dispensing methods.

  • Analyze research data and prepare detailed reports and presentations

  • Drive the continuous improvement of our business to responsibly deliver energy.

  • Work effectively in a team environment with cross-functional members.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Degree in Chemistry or related technical field: PhD or M.S.  with 2+ years of experience, or B.S. with 4+ years of experience.

  • Experience in synthetic chemistry (small molecules or polymers) and advanced characteristic techniques

  • Deep understanding of structure property relationships and how to apply them to solve practical problems

  • Proven capability in developing innovative solutions, including but not limited to product formulation and development.

  • Ability to work with business counterparts to support our customers and new technology adoption.

  • Ability to analyze and interpret incomplete information. 

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Ability to connect and interact with customers to clearly identify their needs and translate those needs into solutions.

  • Experience in transitioning product development from laboratory scale to pilot and production scale

  • Experience in formal Design of Experiments and/or Data Analytics is a plus

  • Detail oriented and excellent time management skills

  • Excellent written, oral, and visual communication skills to communicate effectively with all levels of the organization, both within and outside the business

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

job.title: Technical Professional
job.description:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, matching 401(k) with company matching and more!

job.description:
Job Description

Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

job.title: Optimization Engineer
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

What You Will Do:

  • Support the sales team and client end users for the purpose of optimizing production for both downhole and surface operations by preparing artificial lift recommendations.

  • High computer application literacy ( including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal software systems)

  • Programming skills ( python, sql, java)

  • Prior experience that required excellent communication and organizational skills

We are looking for a Combo Capillary/ESP Spooling Technician at PCS Ferguson located in Odessa, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

  • Earn Field Bonus Potential

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) company matching and more

What You Will Do:

  • Responsible for field equipment installations, troubleshooting and service of equipment.

  • Uses pneumatic and manual hand tools, bands cable/capillary as it is being installed into the well hole.

  • Responsible for the installation of downhole gauge and wireless commutations

  • Demonstrated ability to band or clamp capillary and TEC cable with a Forum banding tool

  • Develop strong relationships with key customers through frequent and professional communication

  • Know and enforce compliance with all safety policies and procedures

  • Completes DOT paperwork as required to include documentation and maintenance of DOT driving log.

  • Maintain truck and trailer per Company standards

Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Motor vehicle record check

  • Drug and alcohol test

  • Criminal background check

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 2 years of relevant field service work experience; preferred in oil & gas.

  • Strong mechanical aptitude, and problem-solving skills

  • Good communication skills, ability to read and write English

  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate a company vehicle

  • Valid Class A driver's license in state of residence required

  • Must have a valid DOT Medical Certification Card

  • Immigration Sponsorship not available for this position

  • Must be available to work overtime and rotational on call duty

Physical Demands:  

  • Must be able to lift and carry 50 lbs regularly.

  • Role is deemed safety sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands and fingers to pick, feel and/or inspect material. Employee would also reach with hands and arms.

  • Employee is required to occasionally walk in customer field locations which may include inclines, uneven surfaces, and a variety of weather conditions.

  • Employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

  • Employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

job.title: Process & Maintenance Coordinator
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Maintenance Lead located in Aberdeen. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

The support of an organisation that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

Comprehensive benefits package & flexible working arrangements that enable balancing work commitments with other priorities

What You Will Do:

The Process & Maintenance Lead is responsible for the safe, reliable, and efficient operation of plant equipment, facilities, and infrastructure.

This role combines engineering oversight with hands-on maintenance leadership, including responsibility for mechanical integrity, inspection and compliance, maintenance planning, contractor management, and project execution.

The position ensures that all maintenance, inspection, and engineering activities are delivered in line with company standards, regulatory requirements, and production needs, while driving continuous improvement in asset reliability, safety, and cost performance.

Safety, Compliance & Engineering Integrity

  • Ensure full compliance with company safety standards, site policies, and relevant legislation (e.g. COMAH, DSEAR, PUWER, PSSR).
  • Maintain accountability for mechanical integrity and ageing plant management.
  • Lead fitness-for-service assessments and ensure appropriate documentation and follow-up actions.
  • Ensure all plant equipment complies with relevant engineering codes and standards (e.g. API, EEMUA).
  • Participate in HAZOP, Process Hazard Analysis (PHA), and Pre-Start-up Safety Reviews (PSSR).
  • Support and manage Management of Change (MoC) processes.

Maintenance & Reliability

  • Plan, schedule, and coordinate all maintenance activities (preventive, corrective, and reactive).
  • Ensure plant equipment is maintained in safe and efficient working order.
  • Maintain and continuously improve the Computerised Maintenance Management System (CMMS), including asset registers and maintenance schedules.
  • Monitor maintenance KPIs (e.g. overdue maintenance, downtime) and drive continuous improvement.
  • Coordinate statutory inspections (e.g. pressure systems, lifting equipment, LEV, bulk tanks).
  • Undertake or support troubleshooting and minor maintenance activities as required.

Projects & Engineering Delivery

  • Lead and support capital projects, plant modifications, and equipment installations.
  • Plan and coordinate plant outages and shutdowns in line with production requirements.
  • Ensure engineering specifications, documentation, and approvals are properly managed.
  • Supervise installation and commissioning of new or modified equipment.

Contractor & Vendor Management

  • Coordinate and oversee third-party contractors undertaking maintenance, inspection, and project work.
  • Ensure safe systems of work, permits, and risk assessments are implemented and followed.
  • Act as Permit to Work Issuer where required.
  • Liaise with vendors on equipment selection, availability, and cost.
  • Support procurement of spares, services, and capital equipment.

Financial & Budget Management

  • Prepare and manage maintenance and capital budgets.
  • Monitor and control expenditure to ensure alignment with financial targets.
  • Raise and manage purchase requisitions and purchase orders.
  • Ensure accurate tracking of maintenance and project costs (labour, materials, contractors).

Systems, Audits & Continuous Improvement

  • Maintain compliance with ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, and site management systems.
  • Ensure maintenance and engineering documentation is audit-ready.
  • Support internal, customer, and regulatory audits.
  • Participate in incident investigations, root cause analysis, and corrective action implementation.
  • Drive continuous improvement in maintenance practices, asset reliability, and energy efficiency.

Qualifications:

  • Degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Chemical, or similar) or equivalent experience
  • Experience in plant engineering and/or maintenance within a process or COMAH environment
  • Strong knowledge of maintenance planning and execution
  • Experience managing contractors and coordinating site activities
  • Understanding of mechanical integrity, inspections, and regulatory compliance
  • Strong communication, coordination, and organisational skills
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and work to deadlines
  • Experience with SAP and/or CMMS systems
  • Project management experience
  • IOSH / NEBOSH or equivalent safety qualification

Physical Demands:

This role requires working in an chemical manufacturing plant.

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

For more news and information, visit SLB.com

Follow us on Facebook at @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, or Instagram at @SLBGlobal.

job.title: Quality Assurance Associate
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration. 

We are looking for a Quality Assurance Associate located in Broken Arrow, OK. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth
  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best
  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company match and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Execute incoming, in-process and final inspections on assemblies and individual components per specifications, and drawings
  • Complete inspection reports, related paperwork, and ensure all information is recorded accurately
  • Follow all safety policies and company-wide safety requirements
  • Identify opportunities and propose solutions for continuous improvement
  • Regularly and predictably attend work and be punctual
  • Identifying defects in parts, approving or rejecting items
  • Checking if products are in compliance with company or engineering identified standards and requirements
  • Identify ongoing quality issues and prevent potential production issues and make suggestions for improvement
  • Maintain quality records as needed
  • Performs other duties as assigned

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • High School education or equivalent
  • Experience in a manufacturing and/or quality environment
  • One year hands on inspection experience in a manufacturing environment
  • Knowledge and skilled in the use of standard measurement techniques commonly used in a manufacturing shop, i.e. calipers, scales, gages, micrometers, meters
  • Attention to detail
  • Time management
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite or related software as needed to complete reports
  • Ability to communicate (read, write, and speak) in English
  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience with Electric Submersible Pump (ESP) equipment
  • Machine shop inspection experience
  • CMM experience

Pre-employment Requirements

  • Criminal background check
  • Drug and alcohol screen

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.
  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
  • Medium work: Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects.
  • Ascending or descending ladders, stairs, scaffolding, ramps, poles and the like, using feet and legs and/or hands and arms. Body agility is emphasized.
  • Maintaining body equilibrium to prevent falling when walking, standing, or crouching on narrow, slippery, or erratically moving surfaces.
  • Bending body downward and forward by bending spine at the waist. This factor is important if it occurs to a considerable degree and requires full use of the lower extremities and back muscles.
  • Bending legs at knee to come to a rest on knees.
  • Bending the body downward and forward by bending leg and spine.
  • Moving about on hands and knees or hands and feet.
  • Extending hand(s) and arm(s) in any direction.
  • Moving about on foot to accomplish tasks, particularly for long distances or moving from one work site to another.
  • Using upper extremities to press against something with steady force in order to thrust forward, downward, or outward.
  • Using upper extremities to exert force in order to draw, drag, haul, or tug objects in a sustained motion.
  • Raising objects from a lower to a higher position or moving objects horizontally from position to position.
  • Picking, pinching, typing, or otherwise working, primarily with fingers rather than the whole hand or arm as in handling.
  • Applying pressure to an object with the fingers and palm.
  • Perceiving attributes of objects, such as size, shape, temperature, or texture by touching with skin, particularly that of fingertips.
  • Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word.  Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to customer or other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly.
  • Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction.  Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound.
  • Substantial movement (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers to use a computer.
  • The employee is required to have close visual acuity to perform an activity such as: visual inspection involving small defects, small parts and/or operation of machines (including inspection), using measurement devices, and/or assembly or fabrication of parts at distances close to the eyes.
  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to operate motor vehicles.
  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to determine the accuracy, neatness, thoroughness of work assigned or to make general observations of facilities or structures.

job.title: Application Engineer I
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Applications Engineer located in Midland, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Develop technical proposals of how the organization's products and services can meet customer needs and be integrated and implemented with the customer's existing systems and equipment.

  • Develop and deliver sales presentations and respond to technical questions regarding the organization's product, system, or service.

  • Estimate the costs, resources, time, and technical requirements of modifying, configuring, and installing the organization's products and services to meet customer needs.

  • Liaise with hardware, software, and systems design engineers to ensure that products and services are modified, configured, and installed according to customer needs.

  • Respond to customer questions and troubleshoot, investigate, and resolve technical problems that arise during or after implementation.

  • Train customers on the features of the product or service they have purchased.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or equivalent experience

  • 2 years of experience

  • Candidate must be authorized to work in the US, no Visa sponsorship offered for this position.

  • Expertise to provide technical advice during larger or difficult negotiations, or with customer who are of strategic importance to the organization.

  • Interact politely and professionally with customers and /or coworkers

  • Strong customer skills including the ability to explain and train.

  • Follow all safety policies and company-wide safety requirements; encourage action assuring safe behavior; confront unsafe behavior and conditions proactively and positively

  • Work in excess of 8 hours per day and 40 hours per week, as required to meet business needs

  • Work in an unsupervised environment

  • Read, write, and speak English fluently

  • Use a computer to communicate, create, and access data

Physical Demands:

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. 

  • Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally and/or a negligible amount of force frequently or constantly to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects, including the human body.

  • Sedentary work involves sitting most of the time.

  • Grasping:  Applying pressure to an object with the fingers and palm.

  • Feeling:  Perceiving attributes of objects, such as size, shape, temperature, or texture by touching with skin, particularly that of fingertips.

  • Talking:  Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word.  Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to customer or other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly.

  • Hearing:  Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction.  Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound.

  • Sitting: May sit for extended periods of time.

  • Moving around the office: Moving between desk and file cabinets and/or office equipment.

  • Repetitive motion:  Substantial movement (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers to use a computer.

  • The employee is required to have close visual acuity to perform an activity such as: preparing and analyzing data and figures; transcribing; viewing a computer terminal;

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to determine the accuracy, neatness, thoroughness of work assigned or to make general observations of facilities or structures.

job.title: Account Manager (Rotational)
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Account Manager, working remotely in a camp setting within the Fort McMurray region.  The Account Manager will be primarily responsible for revenue and profit growth of ChampionX programs and services in targeted accounts within the Oil Sands Region.

You will be a self starter, that has excellent time management skills and a desire to grow your accounts. In addition, you will be responsible for building long-term relationships throughout your customer base by understanding their key business drivers, executing system assurance programs, and selling new technologies.

If you are a passionate sales professional with a proven track record of success in technical sales, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base salary, bonus structure and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • A flexible and independent work environment that fosters a good work-life balance.

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement.

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes extended health benefits, a company-paid pension plan, and more!

  • Company-paid pension plan, employee and family assistance program, paid vacation, sick and personal days.

What You Will Do:

  • Work closely with current and new customers within an assigned territory/customer base.  Understand customer business needs and recommend continuous improvement and innovation plans that will maintain and grow sales.

  • Develop strong relationships with key stakeholders in current and new customer organizations, including plant or facility executives.

  • Generate and execute sales plans in assigned customer base and new customer accounts to meet defined territory profit increase goals.

  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer issues, escalating as required.

  • Engage in problem solving by performing system analysis, interpreting data and providing written recommendations to ensure customer operations are performing at optimal levels.

  • Actively sell and support ChampionX innovations and technology in assigned customers to promote long-term business relationships with ChampionX.

  • Possible travel within assigned sales territory

Position Details:

  • Candidate may reside within Western Canada, with access to air travel, as this position is rotational.

  • This is a 7 days on/ 7 days off rotational schedule.

  • No immigration sponsorship offered.

  • This is a safety-sensitive position, and the successful candidate will be required to complete drug and alcohol testing.

  • This role requires the individual to be clean-shaven to comply with the customer site policy and safety requirements

  • Must have a valid Driver’s License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 5 years of technical sales or field sales support experience.

  • Technical degree, college or bachelor's degree in science or engineering, or a relevant field of study.

  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite, and ability to learn internal business systems)

  • Prior experience that demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multitask

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience in the oil and gas industry preferred

  • Previous Petrochemical experience

  • Water treatment or specialty chemical industry experience

  • Working knowledge of water treatment systems

  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation

  • Prior experience that required excellent communication skills

  • Prior experience that required excellent organizational skills

  • Account leadership experience that includes calling on multilevel plant or facilities management to accomplish company goals

job.title: Production Fitter
job.description:
Primary Responsibilities/Essential Functions:

Essential functions may include the following tasks, knowledge, skills and other characteristics. This list of tasks is illustrative only, and is not a comprehensive listing of all functions and tasks performed by positions in this class perform all of the duties listed, nor does it necessarily list all possible duties that may be assigned.

  • Assemble, manufacture, test or tear down and rebuild artificial lift products according to specifications.
  • Identify, report and problem solve issues arising with the design, or components by following the correct internal processes for reporting issues, faults or non-conformities.
  • Actively participate and oversee the company’s continuous improvement culture by raising, actioning, and closing out CI’s or MOC’s that will improve efficiencies in the plant.
  • Taking corrective actions as required to enhance productivity / efficiency or to minimise adverse impacts.
  • Contribute towards the implementation of lean manufacturing tools and techniques to improve efficiencies and eliminate waste.
  • Contributing towards quality control or manuals, procedures/work instructions, risk assessments or other documentation as required.
  • Establish and maintain high staff morale, model sound leadership and teamwork.
  • Maintains a safe and healthy work environment by following standards and procedures; complying with legal regulations
  • Drives and contributes to the department continuous improvement culture by identifying and suggesting areas for improvement and encouraging team members to do the same.
  • Maintains confidentiality of all matters.

Other duties as required

job.title: Downhole Field Service Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX (SLB) has an immediate need for a Downhole Field Service Technician located in Odessa, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.
  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.
  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement.
  • The chance to join a learning and problem-solving culture
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • The Field Service Technician installs, services, and/or delivers downhole equipment at customer locations under supervision and guidance of Lead Downhole Technicians and/or Local Manager. Operates a truck safely in accordance with Company policy and local, county, state, and federal laws.
  • Performs pre-trip and post-trip inspections of assigned vehicle.
  • Operates various types of vehicles, as assigned, safely, in all types of weather and traffic conditions.
  • Inspects and identifies problems on all assigned equipment.
  • Works in shop to aid in the manufacturing and redressing of products.
  • Field installation of products on customer locations.
  • Loads and unloads vehicle and maintains safety and security of load at all times.
  • Carries out all necessary checks to ensure vehicle is safe and legal and follows appropriate procedures if it is not.
  • Plans and adapts routes as necessary and feedback to immediate supervisor on any issues around load planning and routing.
  • Acts as company ambassador when dealing with customers.
  • Develops good working relationships with customers through professional communication.
  • Maintains proactive contact with suppliers to understand order status, packaging problems, pricing, and other matters.
  • Arrives and signs in on time; works hours as scheduled; wears clean, neat clothing.
  • Loads and unloads cargo at designated stops safely; interacts with staff and customers in a positive manner.
  • Reports all accidents, incidents, or unusual occurrences to dispatch office immediately; submits written reports on accidents, incidents or unusual occurrences as required.
  • Completes all paperwork promptly and accurately as required using any electronic devices that might be required (scanners, hand held POD etc).
  • Follows rules and regulations of the Company, local, county, state, and federal laws.
  • Knows and complies with all safety policies and procedures.
  • Contributes to the department continuous improvement culture.
  • Maintains a clean and well-organized work area.
  • Assists with physical inventories.
  • Maintains confidentiality of all matters.
  • Other duties as required. 

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School diploma or GED or equivalent education and/or experience.
  • 1 years of relevant experience
  • Valid driver's license in state of residence required
  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate company vehicle
  • Immigration sponsorship not available for this role
  • Some computer skills
  • Good verbal and written communication skills
  • Good driving record
  • Basic math and analytical skills
  • Must be self-motivated and disciplined to work autonomously with minimal supervision
  • Good organizational skills with the ability to effectively handle multiple tasks

Physical Demands:  

  • Ability to lift up to 50lbs
  • Role is deemed safety sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing
  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus
  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear
  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms

Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Drug and alcohol test
  • Criminal background check
  • MVR

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience in the oil and gas industry preferred

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. job.title: Chemical Blending Operator
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Chemical Blending Operator located in Leduc, AB.  If you are a dedicated Production Operator with a proven track record of success, we invite you to apply.  The Blending Operator will be primarily responsible for carrying out the assigned plant production schedules in order to meet internal and external customer requirements. 

What’s in it For You:

  • A flexible, independent work environment

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Grow your income as you drive growth

  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety to include top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, a company-paid pension plan, tuition reimbursement and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Progress through the Operator Progression Program within the warehouse, yard and blend floor

  • Ensure the safe and efficient receipt, storage and dispatch of warehouse materials

  • Assist with physical inventory counts and processes

  • Prepare and perform chemical transfers to and from storage tanks, trucks and railcars

  • Safely and reliably blend quality finished products and package bulk, drum and IBC products

  • Enter production tickets, goods receipts, and shipping documentation in a timely manner

  • Inspect and clean the work area daily

  • Perform required routine operator maintenance

  • Comply with all applicable ISO procedures

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in Leduc, AB or the surrounding area.

  • Working hours are Monday-Friday, starting at 6 AM.

  • Ability to work overtime when required.

  • Immigration Sponsorship is not available for this role

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma or equivalent.

Physical Demands:

  • This role is a safety-sensitive position and the successful candidate will be required to complete pre-employment testing in accordance with ChampionX’s policy

  • Ability to push, pull, lift and carry up to 50lbs

  • The physical environment requires the employee to work both inside and outside in heat/cold, wet/humid and dry/arid conditions.

  • Must be clean-shaven upon request, due to safety situations

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Dedicated to safety and quality

  • Forklift experience

  • Rail yard experience, including unloading chemical cars

  • Previous experience with ChampionX as a Blending Operator preferred

  • Chemical manufacturing experience or equivalent industry experience

  • SAP knowledge and experience

  • Demonstrates initiative for self-improvement and assuming additional responsibilities

  • Knowledge of automated control systems

  • Responsible and flexible team player with a positive, “can-do” attitude

  • Intermediate level computer skills

job.title: Assistant Plant Operator
job.description:
Job Description Disclaimer:

SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has future and immediate hiring needs for experienced Manufacturing Blend Operator I (Plant Operator I) professionals in Scott, LA to support our operations team. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering competitive pay, career growth opportunities, and comprehensive benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, technology, and continuous education
  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety, including top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment
  • Pride in working for a company that provides clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments
  • Opportunity for career advancement within a global organization
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, matching 401(k) with company matching, and more

Summary

Responsible for assisting in the safe and efficient operations of plant operations. This includes all aspects of producing, manufacturing, blending, storing, and handling product. Maintains the highest standards of safety, quality, housekeeping, and environmental awareness at all times. Responsible for proper documentation and communication of important information to leadership. This role is safety-sensitive.

What You Will Do:

  • Complete most work functions within production operations
  • Complete general forklift duties and operate yard truck
  • Safely and reliably collect samples and transfer bulk raw materials to and from tankers and storage tanks
  • Safely and reliably package finished products to and from pails, drums, IBCs, SS totes, tankers, and bulk storage tanks
  • Operate mechanical and electrical equipment such as pumps, mixing equipment, filters, and automated control systems used in packaging products
  • Complete and comply with all safety initiatives and certification requirements, including all near miss and incident reporting
  • Be accountable for the data entry of production tickets, goods receipts, and shipping documentation
  • Ensure a clean and organized work environment is always maintained
  • Assist with physical inventory counts and processes
  • Comply with all applicable ISO requirements

Position Details:

  • Location: Scott, LA
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing
  • Ability to work in a manufacturing and plant environment
  • Ability to safely operate forklifts and other plant equipment
  • May require overtime, weekends, or shift work based on operational needs

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • 6 months to 3 years of relevant manufacturing, production, blending, warehouse, or plant operations experience
  • Ability to follow safety procedures and operational guidelines
  • Basic computer and documentation skills
  • Ability to work effectively in a team environment

Physical Demands:

  • Ability to lift/push/pull/carry up to 55 pounds
  • Ability to stand, walk, bend, climb, and work in a manufacturing environment for extended periods
  • Ability to wear required PPE and comply with all safety requirements

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Prior experience in chemical manufacturing or plant operations
  • Forklift certification or prior forklift operating experience
  • Experience working with pumps, blending systems, filters, or automated control systems
  • Experience with inventory processes and production documentation
  • Strong communication, teamwork, and organizational skills

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there. For more news and information, visit SLB.com

Follow us on Facebook at @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, or Instagram at @SLBGlobal.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion 

We believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. We are committed to fair and equal treatment of employees and applicants. We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. In all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement, we will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, disability, or status as a covered veteran.

job.title: Warehouse Specialist
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has an immediate need for a Warehouse Specialist located in Williston, ND. If you are a dedicated warehouse operator with a proven track record of success in logistics, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base hourly rate and benefits. 

What’s in it For You:

·       Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

·       Grow your income as you drive growth

·       Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety to include top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment

·       Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k and more 

·   Compensation: $24-$26/hr with overtime opportunities.

What You Will Do:

This position will primarily be responsible for loading/unloading products leaving from or arriving at warehouse locations.

·       Report to and receive instructions from the Field Operations Manager

·       Operate necessary equipment such as pumps, mules, forklifts, loading platforms

·       Responsible for making proper hookups from storage tanks to trucks and ensuring the correct chemical is loaded

·       Keep safety areas clear and maintain good housekeeping

·       Load/unload trucks containing drums, bulk totes, and other packaged products

·       Responsible for the sampling of chemicals and related paperwork

·       Perform daily inspection on all equipment used

·       Maintain and monitor in-house mixed chemical bulk totes

·       Pick and stage orders for all upcoming shipments

Basic Qualifications:

·       Minimum high school graduate or GED​

·  Must have a clean MVR

·       No immigration sponsorship offered for this position

·       Must be able to speak, read, and write English

Physical Demands:

·       Must be able to lift and carry a minimum of 50 lbs.

·       Role is deemed safety sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

Preferred Qualifications:

·       3 years of experience working in a similar chemical environment

·       Experience with handling products in various types of packages including bulk, drums, bulk totes and mini/micro-feed units

·       Experience performing warehouse duties, which include preventative maintenance duties, inventory and ordering of chemicals, receiving incoming shipments, organizing warehouse for safe and efficient chemical storage, operation of customized chemical manufacturing equipment and the upkeep of warehouse grounds

·       Knowledge of pumps, valves, hand tools, and welding equipment

·       Awareness of chemical safety and the measures to take in the event of skin contact or spill

·       Proficiency with basic Math

·       Basic knowledge of personal computers and Windows software

·       Experience with SAP

About ChampionX

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a

balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators,

that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future

with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going

further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

job.title: Manufacturing Engineering Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of Champion X on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with Champion X and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has an immediate need for a Manufacturing Engineering Technician located in Broken Arrow, OK. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits. 

 

What’s in it For You:  

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth. 

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best. 

  • Thrive in a company that values safety and continuous improvement and encourages career growth. 

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

 

What You Will Do:  

  • Perform technical tasks under the direction of an engineer or manager. 

  • Help support engineering project fabrication, machining, and assembly.

  • Troubleshoot and repair production equipment of various types.

  • Interact regularly with vendors and co-workers.

  • Document assembly steps and maintain records for company and customers.

  • Follow all safety policies and company-wide safety requirements.

  • Read and follow written and/or verbal directions and use a computer to communicate, create, and access information.

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • This safety-sensitive role may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

  • One year experience with the following:

  • Performing mechanical assembly work

  • Operation of manual lathe/mill

  • Welding (GMAW, SMAW, and GTAW)

  • Ability to interpret machine drawings and electrical schematics

  • Basic understanding of single and 3 phase electrical.

  • Working knowledge of hand & power tools, overhead crane

  • Speak, read, and write English fluently.

  • Self-starter with strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

  • Ability to work overtime and Saturdays as required

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Pre-Employment requirements 

  • Drug screen 

  • Background Check 

  • Motor Vehicle Record (MVR) check 

 

Physical Demands:

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.  Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. 

  • Potential travel: up to 20% (domestically) 

  • Medium work: Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects. 

  • Requires Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) such as safety shoes, safety glasses, hardhats hearing protection and respirators. 

  • May stand, walk, stoop, sit, bend, twist, grasp, lift, climb,   

  • Requires visual and hearing acuity  

  • Activities occur indoors and outdoors. 

  • Extreme heat: Temperatures above 100 degrees for periods of more than one hour. Consideration should be given to the effect of other environmental conditions, such as wind and humidity. 

  • Hazards: Includes a variety of physical conditions, such as proximity to moving mechanical parts, moving vehicles, electrical current or exposure to chemicals. 

  • Atmospheric conditions: One or more of the following conditions that affect the respiratory system or the skin:  fumes, odors, dust, mists, gases, or poor ventilation. 

  • Oils: potential air and/or skin exposure to oils and other cutting fluids.

job.title: Sr. Electronics Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration. We have an immediate need for an Lead Electronics Technician. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture, and empowers through continuous improvement

What You Will Do:

  • Troubleshoots and repairs induction heating equipment, including power supplies, inverters, and control systems.

  • Diagnoses and services power electronics systems, including IGBT modules, rectifiers, and inverter circuits.

  • Works safely with high-voltage systems (480V+) and low-voltage controls (24VDC, PLC I/O, instrumentation).

  • Inspects, maintains, and replaces induction coils, ensuring proper alignment, insulation, and cooling performance.

  • Identifies and resolves coil-related issues such as arcing, overheating, scaling, insulation breakdown, and shorting.

  • Maintains and troubleshoots water-cooling systems.

  • Reads and interprets electrical schematics and ladder logic.

  • Troubleshoots PLC systems, sensors, and feedback devices.

  • Maintains spare parts inventory and usage tracking within CMMS (Leading2Lean).

  • Documents work performed in CMMS (e.g., Leading2Lean).

  • Follows all electrical safety procedures, including LOTO and arc flash.

  • Contributes to the department continuous improvement culture.

  • Knows and complies with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Maintains a clean and well-organized work area.

  • Maintains confidentiality of all matters.

  • Other duties as required.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Associate degree or technical certification (or equivalent experience).

  • 3+ years of industrial electronics or maintenance experience.

  • Experience with IGBT-based systems and power electronics.

  • Experience with high- and low-voltage systems.

  • PLC troubleshooting experience.

  • Good oral and written communication skills, including the ability to follow written and oral instructions.

  • Good math and analytical skills.

  • Good organizational and time management skills.

  • Good computer skills, including Microsoft Office Suite.

Other Preferred Skills:

Ability to Troubleshoot the following:

  • Digital multimeters, clamp meters
  • Oscilloscopes (IGBT switching and waveform analysis)
  • •Meggers (insulation testing)
  • IR cameras / thermal imaging
  • Temperature measurement tools (thermocouples, pyrometers)
  • PLC/programming laptops and diagnostic software
  • Cooling system diagnostic tools

Ability to implement the following computer skills:

  • Proficiency using CMMS systems (e.g., Leading2Lean)
  • Ability to use PLC programming software (Allen-Bradley, Siemens)
  • Experience with HMI interfaces
  • Knowledge of industrial networks (EtherNet/IP, Modbus)
  • Ability to navigate electronic manuals and OEM software
  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel, Word, Outlook
  • Comfortable using laptops for diagnostics and system interfacing

Work Environment & Physical Demands:  

  • Work will be performed in industrial manufacturing environments

  • Exposure to dirty, dusty, oily, and greasy conditions

  • Exposure to both hot and cold environments, including high-heat areas near induction equipment

  • Ability to work around moving machinery, electrical systems, and water-cooled equipment

  • Must be able to lift up to 50 lbs and perform physically demanding tasks

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • The employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

  • The employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.


We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

job.title: Journeyman Electrician/Automation Field Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are searching for a Journeyman Electrician/Automation Field Technician for future opportunities in the Permian Basin.

What You Will Do:

  • Works under general supervision to perform moderately complex customer support activities. Assists with on-site installations, check-out, and emergency repairs.

  • Uses mastery of electrician trades skills and knowledge to diagnose and solve electrical problems and installation of Spirit Smarten Controllers and other related equipment.

  • Creates and assembles components and installs them as required.

  • Performs preventative maintenance.

  • Documents maintenance and repairs as required.

  • Interacts fluently with those in other trades and work areas to complete work assignments; provides technical electrical expertise to mechanics, facilities, and other work teams.

  • Contributes to the department continuous improvement culture.

  • Knows and complies with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Maintains a clean and well-organized work area.

  • Other duties as required.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma or GED

  • Licensed Journeyman Electrician

  • 4 years of experience

  • Can perform moderately complex pulls and installs independently

  • Strong customer relations skills including the ability to explain and train

  • Knowledge of proper use of hand tools

  • Knowledge of wiring and cabling

  • Analytical and problem-solving ability

  • Ability to follow prescribed company policies, procedures, and protocols for installation, service and equipment maintenance

  • Interact politely and professionally with customers and/or coworkers

  • Ability to effectively present information and respond to questions from managers, clients, and customers

  • Ability to effectively plan and organize

  • Follow all safety policies and company-wide safety requirements; encourage action assuring safe behavior; confront unsafe behavior and conditions proactively and positively

  • Work more than 8 hours per day and 40 hours per week, as required to meet business needs

  • Work in an unsupervised environment

  • Use a computer to communicate, create, and access data

  • Determine the priorities, goals, and objectives of work assigned

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.

  • Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects.

  • Climbing:  Ascending or descending ladders, stairs, scaffolding, ramps, poles, and the like, using feet and legs and/or hands and arms. Body agility is emphasized.

  • Balancing:  Maintaining body equilibrium to prevent falling when walking, standing, or crouching on narrow, slippery, or erratically moving surfaces.

  • Stooping:  Bending body downward and forward by bending spine at the waist. This factor is important if it occurs to a considerable degree and requires full use of the lower extremities and back muscles.

  • Kneeling:  Bending legs at knee to come to a rest on knees.

  • Crawling:  Moving about on hands and knees or hands and feet.

  • Reaching:  Extending hand(s) and arm(s) in any direction.

  • Walking:  Moving about on foot to accomplish tasks, particularly for long distances or moving from one work site to another.

  • Pushing:  Using upper extremities to press against something with steady force in order to thrust forward, downward, or outward.

  • Pulling:  Using upper extremities to exert force to draw, drag, haul, or tug objects in a sustained motion.

  • Lifting:  Raising objects from a lower to a higher position or moving objects horizontally from position to position.

  • Grasping:  Applying pressure to an object with the fingers and palm.

  • Feeling:  Perceiving attributes of objects, such as size, shape, temperature, or texture by touching with skin, particularly that of fingertips.

  • Talking:  Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word.  Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to customer or other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly.

  • Hearing:  Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction.  Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound.

  • Repetitive motion:  Substantial movement (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers to use a computer.

  • The employee is required to have close visual acuity to perform an activity such as: visual inspection involving small defects, small parts and/or operation of machines (including inspection), using measurement devices, and/or assembly or fabrication of parts at distances close to the eyes.

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to operate motor vehicles.

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to determine the accuracy, neatness, thoroughness of work assigned or to make general observations of facilities or structures.

  • Indoor and outdoor environmental conditions.  Activities occur indoors and outdoors.

  • Extreme heat.  Temperatures above 100 degrees for periods of more than one hour.  Consideration should be given to the effect of other environmental conditions, such as wind and humidity.

  • Hazards.  Includes a variety of physical conditions, such as proximity to moving mechanical parts, moving vehicles, electrical current or exposure to chemicals.

  • Atmospheric conditions.  One or more of the following conditions that affect the respiratory system or the skin:  fumes, odors, dust, mists, gases, or poor ventilation.

  • Oils. There is air and/or skin exposure to oils and other cutting fluids.

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.
