job.title: Dalton Highway HazMat CDL Driver
job.description:
Job Description:

ChampionX has an immediate need for an Operational Specialist in Anchorage, AK or Fairbanks, AK.  If you are a dedicated DOT driving professional with a proven track record of success in customer delivery, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base hourly rate and benefits. 

This position will primarily be responsible for providing chemical treatments to oil and gas wells, and the maintenance and safe operation of any type of vehicle used to deliver chemicals to customers, plants and/or warehouse locations. This position will be responsible for the transportation of chemicals to Prudhoe Bay on a regular basis.

What's in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits
  • Access to best in class resources, tools, and technology
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection
  • Comprehensive benefits package (Medical, Dental, Vision, 401K and much more) that starts Day 1
  • $10,000 Hiring Bonus

What You Will Do:

  • Chemical treating of all scheduled wells and locations on a regular basis
  • Maintain chemical inventory reports
  • Present a professional manner and work closely with salespeople to satisfy customer requirements.
  • Maintain regular communication with sales and supervisor in order to report field problems and/or safety hazards
  • Maintain truck and equipment in a safe and operable condition by performing daily and periodic mechanical and safety inspections
  • Maintain driver's hours of service by using Turnpike and/or paper logbook reporting
  • Keeps current on safety techniques and take advantage of all opportunities to improve driving and safety skills
  • Recognize and avoid safety hazards in the work environment
  • Properly load trucks, prepare daily bills of lading, schedule the order of daily treatments and submit treating reports to sales

Territory / Location Information:

  • This position will be responsible for chemical treatment and delivery between Anchorage, Fairbanks, and Prudhoe Bay. This position will require weekly Haul Road routes.
  • Candidate must reside within 50 miles of Anchorage or Fairbanks, AK
  • Relief travel positions also available with a $2.00 an hour travel bonus

Schedule:

  • Monday Thru Friday with an occasional Saturday
  • 10+ Hour shifts
  • Overnight and Multiple Day trips on Haul Road
  • Some regional travel as needed
  • Part-time available

Basic/Minimum Qualifications:

  • Minimum high school graduate or GED
  • Must currently hold a valid Class A Commercial's Drivers License, issued by the state in which the candidate resides
  • Must have HazMat and Tanker Endorsements or be willing to be obtain within 30 days
  • One (1) year of commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past three years required
  • Acceptable motor vehicle record (3 years)
  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate company vehicle
  • All candidates must have the ability to speak, read and write English
  • Immigration sponsorship is not offered for this role
  • Must be comfortable working with cameras in the truck cab
  • No manual transmission restrictions

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Carry up to 50 pounds chest high.
  • Must be able to pass a drug screen and physical exam

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Three (3) years commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past five years required
  • Experience with the operation of forklifts, drum dollies, trailer hoists, chemical pumps, hoses, meters, printers and gauges
  • Experience with handling products in various types of packages including bulk, drums, Porta-Feeds and mini/micro-feed units
  • Knowledge of precautions needed if exposed to H2S (hydrogen sulfide)
  • Experience in customer relations
  • Experience performing warehouse duties, which include preventative maintenance, inventory and ordering of chemicals, receiving incoming shipments, organizing warehouse for safe and efficient chemical storage, operation of customized chemical manufacturing equipment and the upkeep of warehouse grounds
  • Awareness of chemical safety and the measures to take in the event of skin contact or spill

Position Compensation and Benefits

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!
  • Hourly and overtime pay earning potential between $70,000 to $95,000 per year!
     

About ChampionX

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a

balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators,

that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future

with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going

further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

job.title: Assistant Process Operator
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration. 

We are looking for an Assistant Process Operator in our Sugar Land, TX plant. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. 

What’s In It for You:  

  • Enjoy paid training allowing you to learn from successful professionals 
  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology 
  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, and drives a safety-focused culture 
  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth 
  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best 
  • Access to Employee Resource Groups (ERG’s), facilitating collaboration and fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. 
  • Utilization of multiple knowledge-sharing platforms to enhance collaboration and engagement across teams. 
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more! 

  

What You Will Do:  

  • Follow safety policies to provide a safe workplace for all employees and supports the guiding principles of Goal Zero to continually improve the plant’s safety, health and environmental performance, security, design, development, manufacture, storage, marketing, distribution, use and ultimate safe disposal of chemical products. Drive culture of safety; Report safety observations / incidents  
  • Supports and is committed to the Quality Improvement Process.  Plans strategies to ensure error-free work by “doing it right the first time” and conveys this attitude in the daily performance of various duties  
  • Identify areas for process improvement within the roles function supporting continuous improvement  
  • Be proficient in use of process equipment  
  • Perform all aspects of manufacturing a product from a process order or bill of material 
  • Operate of PC to perform various production functions such as generating process orders, inventory checks, label making, and some quality checks 
  • Prepare drums, pails, totes, and other containers for transport of products  
  • Maintains a clean, safe, and environmentally compliant work and storage area 
  • Labels and placards packaged material in accordance with company policy and government regulations  
  • Operate pumps, valves, and other processing equipment for safe and efficient chemical production 
  • Operate all transporting equipment used to receive raw materials and the transport of products  
  • Effectively load and unload bulk materials from tankers  
  • Make necessary hose line-ups for loading material from tankers into vessels and vice versa  
  • Operate scales and maintain drumming platform  
  • Support the processes to meet the requirements of the current ISO and RC Standards 
  • Prepare drums and toes for loading and shipping which includes labeling of the container 
  • Participate in physical inventory, safety meetings, and improvements committees 
  • May perform necessary Quality Assurance tests 
  • Be able to act as a second line Production Planner as needed 
  • Be able to troubleshoot and repair minor problems and alert maintenance to major repairs 
  • Demonstrate successful ability to supervise Assistant Process Operator and Operator 
  • Effectively manage through “hand on” example 
  • Have final responsibility for production moving out of the plant in an efficient manner, maintaining a 95% on time service level 

Minimum Qualifications: 

  • High school diploma/GED 
  • Minimum 1 year manufacturing experience  
  • Must have the ability to don a respirator  
  • Must have the ability to wear fall protection if necessary 
  • Willing and able to work weekends 
  • Willing and able to work a rotation and nights 
  • Excellent written and verbal communications skills 
  • Team Player with ability to perform physical work. 
  • All candidates must have the ability to speak, read and write English 
  • Immigration sponsorship is not offered for this role 

Physical Demands: 

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Carry up to 50 pounds chest high, climb stairs or ladders, and agile enough to squat and bend over (Agility Tests 1, 2a, 2b). 
  • Role is deemed safety sensitive and is subject to employer or customer drug testing. 

Preferred Qualifications: 

  • Process Technology Degree preferred 
  • Transportation experience  
  • Forklift experience 

About Us:  

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.  

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

job.title: Plunger Lift Automation Service Representative
job.description:

ChampionX (SLB) has an immediate need for a Plunger Lift Automation Service Representative located in El Reno, Oklahoma. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement.

What You Will Do:

  • Review quotes, installation and repair plans to ensure a thorough understanding of customer needs and the parts and process required to address the need.

  • As needed, explains installation, service or repair plans, and invoices to customers; discusses cost or time overruns with team leader or manager.

  • Executes installations, repair, or service plans and documents results.

  • Creates product failure analysis reports.

  • Develops good working relationships with customers through professional communication.

  • Develop and maintain application competency in Production Automation. Automation competencies include computer skills in SCADA, telemetry, Windows operating environment, database technologies, Internet, and networking.  Wireless communication competency required (SCADA, telemetry, host systems, etc.).

  • Ensure an accurate account of all truck inventory.

  • Knows and complies with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Ability to work weekends and overtime as required.

  • Other duties as required.

Minimum Experience:

  • Four years of relevant experience in the oil and gas industry.

  • Valid driver's license

  • Strong mechanical skills - Perform mechanical field maintenance work to ensure optimal availability and reliability of artificial lift equipment.

  • Good oral and written communication skills - Listens to understand input, feedback and concerns. Responds timely and effective.

  • Good analytical and problem-solving skills - Identifies and resolves problems. Develops alternative solutions; Works well in group problem solving situations; Uses reason even when dealing with emotional topics.

  • Good math skills

  • Good organizational and time management skills, with the ability to work with minimal supervision - Prioritizes and plans work activities; Uses time efficiently; Plans for additional resources

  • Good computer skills, including Microsoft Office suite

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

  • Motor vehicle record check

  • Drug and alcohol test

  • Criminal background check

Physical Demands:

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • Employee is required to occasionally walk which may include inclines, uneven surfaces, and a variety of weather conditions.

job.title: Journeyman Electrician/Automation Field Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are searching for a Journeyman Electrician/Automation Field Technician for future opportunities in the South Texas region.

What You Will Do:

  • Works under general supervision to perform moderately complex customer support activities. Assists with on-site installations, check-out, and emergency repairs.

  • Uses mastery of electrician trades skills and knowledge to diagnose and solve electrical problems and installation of Spirit Smarten Controllers and other related equipment.

  • Creates and assembles components and installs them as required.

  • Performs preventative maintenance.

  • Documents maintenance and repairs as required.

  • Interacts fluently with those in other trades and work areas to complete work assignments; provides technical electrical expertise to mechanics, facilities, and other work teams.

  • Contributes to the department continuous improvement culture.

  • Knows and complies with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Maintains a clean and well-organized work area.

  • Other duties as required.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma or GED

  • Licensed Journeyman Electrician

  • 4 years of experience

  • Have valid driver's license and meet company driving standards

  • Can perform moderately complex pulls and installs independently

  • Strong customer relations skills including the ability to explain and train

  • Knowledge of proper use of hand tools

  • Knowledge of wiring and cabling

  • Analytical and problem-solving ability

  • Ability to follow prescribed company policies, procedures, and protocols for installation, service and equipment maintenance

  • Interact politely and professionally with customers and/or coworkers

  • Ability to effectively present information and respond to questions from managers, clients, and customers

  • Ability to effectively plan and organize

  • Follow all safety policies and company-wide safety requirements; encourage action assuring safe behavior; confront unsafe behavior and conditions proactively and positively

  • Work more than 8 hours per day and 40 hours per week, as required to meet business needs

  • Work in an unsupervised environment

  • Use a computer to communicate, create, and access data

  • Determine the priorities, goals, and objectives of work assigned

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.

  • Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects.

  • Climbing:  Ascending or descending ladders, stairs, scaffolding, ramps, poles, and the like, using feet and legs and/or hands and arms. Body agility is emphasized.

  • Balancing:  Maintaining body equilibrium to prevent falling when walking, standing, or crouching on narrow, slippery, or erratically moving surfaces.

  • Stooping:  Bending body downward and forward by bending spine at the waist. This factor is important if it occurs to a considerable degree and requires full use of the lower extremities and back muscles.

  • Kneeling:  Bending legs at knee to come to a rest on knees.

  • Crawling:  Moving about on hands and knees or hands and feet.

  • Reaching:  Extending hand(s) and arm(s) in any direction.

  • Walking:  Moving about on foot to accomplish tasks, particularly for long distances or moving from one work site to another.

  • Pushing:  Using upper extremities to press against something with steady force in order to thrust forward, downward, or outward.

  • Pulling:  Using upper extremities to exert force to draw, drag, haul, or tug objects in a sustained motion.

  • Lifting:  Raising objects from a lower to a higher position or moving objects horizontally from position to position.

  • Grasping:  Applying pressure to an object with the fingers and palm.

  • Feeling:  Perceiving attributes of objects, such as size, shape, temperature, or texture by touching with skin, particularly that of fingertips.

  • Talking:  Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word.  Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to customer or other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly.

  • Hearing:  Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction.  Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound.

  • Repetitive motion:  Substantial movement (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers to use a computer.

  • The employee is required to have close visual acuity to perform an activity such as: visual inspection involving small defects, small parts and/or operation of machines (including inspection), using measurement devices, and/or assembly or fabrication of parts at distances close to the eyes.

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to operate motor vehicles.

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to determine the accuracy, neatness, thoroughness of work assigned or to make general observations of facilities or structures.

  • Indoor and outdoor environmental conditions.  Activities occur indoors and outdoors.

  • Extreme heat.  Temperatures above 100 degrees for periods of more than one hour.  Consideration should be given to the effect of other environmental conditions, such as wind and humidity.

  • Hazards.  Includes a variety of physical conditions, such as proximity to moving mechanical parts, moving vehicles, electrical current or exposure to chemicals.

  • Atmospheric conditions.  One or more of the following conditions that affect the respiratory system or the skin:  fumes, odors, dust, mists, gases, or poor ventilation.

  • Oils. There is air and/or skin exposure to oils and other cutting fluids.

job.title: Sales Account Manager - Artificial Lift
job.description:
SLB is seeking a Technical Software Sales Representative

responsible for executing the regional sales strategy for the Texas Region.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

Key Responsibilities

  • Work closely with current and new customers to understand business needs and recommend solutions that assist the customer in meeting their productivity objectives, and improvements that will maintain and grow sales within assigned territory.

  • Partner with Software Engineers / Developers to deliver technical presentation that provide solutions to the customers through XSPOC or other software solutions.

  • Engage in problem solving by performing system analysis, interpreting data and providing written recommendations to ensure customer operations are performing at optimal levels.

  • Actively sell and support software innovations and technology in assigned customers to promote long-term business relationships with SLB.

  • Assist in developing strong relationships with key stakeholders in current and new customers, including plant or facility executives

  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer complaints, escalating as required.

  • Support sales forecasting and market data driven sales strategy.

  • Maintain and updates all sales activity data through Salesforce.com.

  • Mentor other Sales Representatives and Customer Sales Engineers, with a career interest in leading teams.  

Skills/Knowledge:

  • Strong sales network within software for artificial lift, with a proven success in acquiring new customers.

  • Strategic mindset, with the ability to develop sales strategies, analytical and implementation skills.

  • Strong communication, interpersonal and organizational skills including the ability to present and persuade at the leadership level.

  • Excellent negotiation skills.

  • Competitive attitude and strong work ethic with the ability to enthusiastically represent the company.

  • Experience and Knowledge of Automation, Enterprise Software and IIOT for the Oil & Gas industry.

  • Knowledge of Artificial Lift and Well Optimization

  • Familiarity with Oil and Gas control and measurement systems

  • Familiarity with Variable Frequency Drive/Variable Speed Drive integration and control

  • Self-driven motivated

  • Available for inter-regional travel up to 50%.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree

  • 2 years sales experience in Oil & Gas segment with strategic connections

  • Preferred software sales experience for artificial lift system sales experience

  • Strong negational skills

  • Ability to develop effective business relationships

  • Exceptional verbal, written, and presentation skills.

  • Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team.

  • Salesforce (or other CRM) Experience and Oracle.

  • Availability for inter-regional travel 50%.

job.title: Regional Sales Manager
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Regional Sales Manager for Windrock located in Hobbs, NM to create and execute regional channel strategy for its new line of wireless sensors as it applies to customers and applications.

This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

Customer Management:

  • Identify customer’s needs, vision cast, and sales opportunities to drive growth

  • Identify and establish business relationships

  • Listen, understand, and convey customer requirements

  • Identify and leverage resources to perform technical / product training for customers at targeted accounts

  • Identify and communicate customer requirements for new products/solutions

Strategy & Planning:

  • Define a regional strategy and implement its execution for years one, two, and three

  • Identify and develop strategic account plans leveraging other channel partner (internal and external) resources

  • Drive sales through direct customer relationships, distributors, and other ChampionX entities

  • Work closely with the organization to ensure that product strategies and performance are meeting growth and Annual Operating Plan targets

  • Work closely with Marketing & Technology teams to understand pros and cons of various solution approaches

Execution:

  • Articulate value story, technology and product positioning to both business and technical users

  • Create incremental demand, opportunities, and close orders

  • Maintain expert level of product and application knowledge within the battery-operated wireless sensor market

  • Meet sales goal targets monthly, quarterly, and yearly

  • Actively manage channel partners effectiveness, prospecting, closing, forecasting, and KPI’s by using a metrics-based approach to report progress and highlight potential roadblocks with proposed mitigation strategies

  • Maintain accurate records of target accounts, opportunities, quotes, projects, contacts and correspondence in company approved databases through our Salesforce CRM

  • Other duties as assigned

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering, business, or related field

  • Good overall knowledge of the Oil and Gas Industry

  • 5 years of experience in condition monitoring and wireless sensor applications

  • Self-driven work ethic

  • Strong negotiation skills

  • Excellent communication skills

  • Strong Interpersonal and Organizational skills

  • Strong computer skills, including Microsoft Office suite

  • Competitive attitude and strong work ethic with the ability to enthusiastically represent the company

  • Availability for inter-regional travel as required, 50-70%

  • No immigration sponsorship available for this position

    

Physical Demands

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms

job.title: Shop Technician - Gas Lift
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Gas Lift Shop Technician located in Odessa, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s In It For You:

  • First shift generally starts at 5:00 am and may be 8, 10 or 12 hours depending on the workload (with some weekend overtime as needed)

  • Thrive in a company that values safety and continuous improvement and encourages career growth

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Attend to breakdowns, diagnose faults, and oversee equipment improvements and time critical repairs; maintain preventative maintenance and breakdown report and part usage records; investigate the root cause and recommend course of action.

  • Assists shop on problem identification, trouble shooting and equipment issue resolution and repair. Evaluate non-conforming products to determine appropriate requirements to rework, repair, or scrap items.

  • Oversee spare parts inventory and may use a computerized maintenance management system.

  • Coordinate and direct contractors for specialized repairs and installations; maintain good working relations with internal customers and contractors.

  • Maintain toolkit, supplies, and requisite tool inventory.

  • Conduct facility and equipment inspections as a part of the Emergency Response Procedures.

  • Lead and implement Lean 5S + 1 project as a role model.

  • Know and comply with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Utilize hand or power tools and heavy equipment, such as, forklifts and pallet jacks in a safe, reliable manner.

  • Assist with inventory counts of current stock levels to identify potential shortages or excesses.

  • Complete inspections, repair of parts and routine maintenance on capillary trailers.

  • Deliver parts to technicians on job sites.

Pre-Employment requirements

  • Drug screen

  • Background Check

  • Motor Vehicle Check

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • High school diploma or GED

  • 2 years of mechanical shop experience

  • Valid drivers license

  • This safety-sensitive role may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

  • Read, understand and speak English

  • Must be willing to work overtime and Saturdays as required


 

Physical Demands:

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.

  • Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Medium work:  Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects.

  • Requires Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) such as safety shoes and safety glasses

  • May stand, walk, stoop, sit, bend, twist, grasp, lift, climb,  

  • Requires visual and hearing acuity

  • Work primarily occurs in a heated and air-conditioned manufacturing environment

  • Hazards include proximity to moving mechanical parts, moving vehicles, electrical current or exposure to chemicals

  • Atmospheric conditions that affect the respiratory system or skin may include fumes, odors, dust, mists, gases, or poor ventilation

  • Potential skin exposure to oils and other cutting fluids

job.title: Gas Lift Service Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Gas Lift Service Technician located in Tyler, TX, supporting PCS Ferguson Gas Lift product line. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • Earn Field Job Bonus Opportunity

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and has been recognized as one of the most ethical companies in the world

  • Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Provides technical support for the gas lift product line.

  • Reviews quotes, installation and repair plans to ensure a thorough understanding of customer needs and the parts and process required to address the need.

  • As needed, explains installation, service or repair plans, and invoices to customers; discusses cost or time overruns with team leader or manager.

  • Executes installations, repair, or service plans and documents results.

  • Creates product failure analysis reports.

  • Develops good working relationships with customers through professional communication.

  • Operates, recommends and maintains packer and gas lift products and services to meet customer need. This includes, but is not limited to, operating various styles of packers, including assembling, troubleshooting, and releasing of packers.

  • Comprehends a tubing tally and properly fills out installation reports for our customers.

  • Executes tubing set and wireline set packers to company expectations.

  • Performs initial completions, remedial, or downhole maintenance operations from an elevated position.

  • Maintains and performs preventative maintenance and basic repairs to workover rigs and associated equipment, checks fluid, oil, water, hydraulic, and transmission levels.

  • Understands and uses various completion tools complementing the packer product line.

  • Coordinate’s assignments or projects to achieve maximum utilization of clients’ drilling and oil recovery efforts.

  • Work independently at remote customer locations.

  • Troubleshoots and make recommendations for well flow enhancement.

Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Motor vehicle record check

  • Drug and alcohol test

  • Criminal background check

  • Education and certification check

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma/GED

  • 2 years’ equivalent field tech work experience

  • Good technical/mechanical aptitude

  • Strong troubleshooting skills

  • Excellent customer service skills

  • Valid driver's license in state of residence required

  • 21 years of age to operate company vehicle

Physical Demands:

  • Must be able to lift and carry 50 lbs regularly, and 100 lbs occasionally.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • Employee is required to occasionally walk in customer field locations which may include inclines, uneven surfaces, and a variety of weather conditions.

  • Employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

  • Employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

  • Oracle experience

job.title: Assistant Plant Operator
job.description:
Disclaimer

SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

About the Role

ChampionX has an immediate need for an Assistant Plant Operator in our Chemical Technologies group located in Odessa, TX. This position will join the production team in the Odessa plant and allow you to learn and progress through all operational skills required to blend, react, and package products within the facility. The role reports to the Production Team Leads and works closely with other core functional groups within the Odessa ChampionX Manufacturing Plant.

If you are a dedicated professional with a proven track record of success, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering competitive base rates and comprehensive benefits.

What’s in it for You

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, technology, and continuous learning opportunities
  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety, including industry-leading safety training and personal protective equipment
  • The opportunity to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth
  • Support from an organization that believes diverse people, perspectives, and ideas drive better results
  • Pride in working for a company focused on clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments
  • Comprehensive benefits package starting on Day 1, including medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company match, and more

What You Will Do

  • Complete work functions within production operations on a daily basis
  • Responsible for quality blending of finished products and the bulk, drum, and packaging of products
  • Perform general forklift duties as required
  • Operate mechanical and electrical equipment including pumps, mixing equipment, and automation control systems used in blending and packaging operations
  • Complete and comply with all safety initiatives and certification requirements, including near-miss and incident reporting
  • Accountable for data entry of production tickets, goods receipts, and shipping documentation
  • Conduct daily inspections of work areas and complete monthly reporting requirements
  • Maintain daily housekeeping standards to ensure a clean and organized work environment at all times
  • Assist with physical inventory counts and inventory processes
  • Ensure compliance with all applicable ISO requirements

Position Details

  • Location: Odessa, TX
  • Department: Chemical Technologies Manufacturing Operations
  • Reports To: Production Team Leads
  • Schedule: Plant Operations Schedule
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

Minimum Qualifications

  • High School Diploma or GED
  • Must be medically qualified to wear a respirator
  • Physically able to lift up to 60 pounds, climb stairs and ladders, and perform squatting and bending activities (Agility Tests 1, 2a & 2b)
  • Immigration sponsorship is not offered for this role

Preferred Qualifications

  • Team player with the ability to perform physical work in a manufacturing environment
  • Basic computer skills with the ability to navigate core business programs
  • Two-year Process Technology degree preferred
  • Minimum of one (1) year of experience as an operator in a batch or specialty chemical manufacturing plant
  • Minimum of one (1) year of relevant manufacturing or production experience
  • Forklift operation certification preferred
  • 24-Hour HAZWOPER certification preferred

Physical Demands

  • Ability to lift, push, pull, and carry up to 60 pounds
  • Ability to climb stairs and ladders regularly
  • Ability to bend, squat, kneel, and perform repetitive physical activities
  • Must be able to wear required personal protective equipment, including a respirator

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that has been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy and less carbon. Our diverse and innovative teams are focused on advancing technology and developing the new energy solutions needed to meet tomorrow’s challenges.

For more news and information, visit SLB.com.

Follow us on Facebook at @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, and Instagram at @SLBGlobal.

job.title: Chemistry Integration Scientist
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Staff Scientist for our Production Chemicals Technology team, located in Sugar Land, TX. This role will drive the chemistry and product sourcing efforts in support of the Production Systems, Well Construction and Reservoir Performance businesses. 

The ideal candidate is highly versatile, communicates effectively, and thrives in a collaborative environment. If you are a passionate professional that likes to work in an autonomous, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth
  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best
  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and has been recognized as one of the most ethical companies in the world
  • Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Perform work in a safe manner while identifying opportunities for improving safety.
  • Execute on projects, experiments and research of critical chemical components/products with high complexity along with high impact on the business.
  • Collaborate with multiple SLB divisions and business stakeholders/functions to determine best synthetic routes for cost effective scale up and deployment.
  • Collaborate with cross-division technical groups to qualify materials for their respective applications.
  • Demonstrate full use and application of scientific, project management, design excellence, and other principles, theories and concepts to assignments where precedents may exist plus working knowledge of other related disciplines.
  • Provide solutions to a wide range of chemistry challenges associated with various drilling and completion applications via structure property relationships and foundational chemistry/engineering approaches.
  • Demonstrate initiative and creativity in identifying and trying new and different approaches.
  • Demonstrate the ability to translate understanding of chemistry/product compositions into viable products that are able to be manufactured internally.
  • Provide project or technical leadership for strategic sourcing projects. May supervise others or serve as a technical resource and mentor in area of expertise. 
  • Demonstrate continuous improvement mindset and ability to modify processes to improve internal efficiency.
  • Manage time effectively and prioritize assigned tasks to ensure delivery.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Degree in chemistry, chemical engineering, or related technical field.
  • PhD and 5+ years of experience, MS and 7 years minimum experience, BS and 10 years minimum experience.
  • Experience in organic chemistry with the ability to synthesize a wide range of chemistries, including small molecules, surfactants, polymers and other related materials.
  • Problem solver with ability to lead change, manage ambiguity, and be able to adapt to new situations and projects quickly.
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills.  Ability to connect and collaborate with a wide range of stakeholders. 
  • Demonstrated project management and organizational skills. 
  • Immigration VISA sponsorship will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience with the use and application of chemical additives for oilfield related products (drilling fluids, fracturing & stimulation fluids, cement, completion fluids, production chemicals and others).
  • Formulation experience developing new products and improving existing products.
  • Prior experience with product-scale up, commercialization, or manufacturing process troubleshooting.

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

job.title: Outside Sales Account Manager
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the aquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Outside Sales Account Manager located in Midland, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • Flexible, independent work environment where you will manage a monthly schedule

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Grow your income as you drive growth

  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety to include top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Effectively develop and execute regional sales plans to support the strategic growth objectives while maintaining a profitable gross-margin for the Permian region in West Texas.
  • Deliver annual, quarterly and monthly sales in the region with high levels of accountability, integrity and ethics.
  • Set-up customer appointments to conduct sales presentations to promote, sell, and apply our complete product line in a regional sales area Permian - West Texas.
  • Manage assigned accounts as well as developing new accounts, projects or other sales opportunities necessary to meet the revenue goals of an assigned geo-region and associated territories.
  • Create/maintain customers relationships for new or repetitive business.
  • Collaborate, supervise, manage, and provide performance feedback to Sales Representatives and Manufacturing Representatives/Distributors for the area of responsibility.
  • Communicate with the Sales Team and the Production Team regularly to guarantee that sales and client objectives are met.
  • Develop annual marketing strategy in conjunction with the Sales Manager and the inside sales team and identify joint marketing opportunities where applicable.
  • Perform market assessments, competitor analysis, pricing strategies and execute counter strategies to gain market share.
  • Draw conclusions from market assessment data on the competitive environment and the company’s strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and targets, and provide ideas and suggestions to the Sales Manager.
  • Develop a strategic sales plan that identifies prospects and customers, prioritizes them according to importance and ability to provide results.
  • Provide product training at the field and corporate levels for all customer types
  • Evaluate and implement appropriate sales techniques to increase regional sales volume
  • Submit sales activity report as required.
  • Attend relevant product conventions and sales seminars.
  • Manage a geo-region, to deliver sales revenues, customer support and channel management objectives as defined by the Sales Manager.
  • Provide customers with technical support and expertise in reviewing specifications and applying the products.
  • Manage sales, profit margins and operational expenses for designated sales territory.
  • Develop and execute meaningful growth strategies that will deliver incremental & repeatable sales revenues.
  • Develop, update and communicate reliable sales forecasts.
  • Identify, qualify and convert project and new customer sales opportunities that will contribute to the territory revenues.
  • Promote integrity and ethical behavior with customers, partners and employees according to local and broad company policies.
  • Develop install base in adjacent segments to diversify market share in other regions outside of oil and gas

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business, or similar.
  • 5 years of experience in Sales of similar control valve products and accessories.
  • Experience selling through channel partners and distributors.
  • Willingness to travel up to 50-75% in the specified sales territory
  • Must be proficient in using Microsoft Office Suite which includes Word, Excel, PowerPoint.
  • Results oriented, driven, motivated and accountable with good follow up skills to ensure strategy implementation.
  • Contract negotiation experience with strong sales acumen and channel management skills.
  • Adept at managing multiple tasks simultaneously.
  • Provide leadership through example and adequate direction.
  • Ability to produce complex sales reports.
  • Ability to communicate with customers at all levels.
  • Ability to build relationship with company and customer contacts.
  • Self-motivated, driven to meet goals and ability to empathize with customer needs to provide solutions.
  • Ability to build relationships at the manufacturing sites, build trust and be actively engaged in problem solving.
  • Ability to be a strong team player and promote collaboration, sharing of best practices towards the larger strategic business goals.

Physical Demands:

  • Minimal crouching, kneeling, crawling, squatting, climbing ladders, balancing, lifting up to 25 lbs, reaching, twisting, stretching, pushing, pulling, handling, grasping, holding, turning, and dialing up to 10%
  • Occasional bending, climbing stairs and standing between 10% and 33%.
  • Frequent use of a keyboard and mouse, viewing a computer monitor, viewing a cell phone screen, texting, swiping, sitting, walking, driving a motor vehicle and writing between 33% and 66%.
  • Constant hearing, seeing and speaking greater than 66%.
  • Work environment may include home office, vehicle, customer field or office sites, and office cubicle.

About Us:

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

job.title: Class 1 Truck Driver
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Truck Driver located in Leduc, AB.  The Truck Driver is responsible for providing customer delivery of bulk chemicals throughout British Columbia, Alberta and Saskatchewan.  

The Specialist safely operates multiple configuration bulk trucks over congested city highways, rolling and mountainous terrain and rural roads. The Specialist also provides preventative maintenance of any type of vehicle used to deliver chemicals to customers, plants and warehouse locations.

If you are a passionate professional that likes to work in an autonomous, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes extended health benefits, a company-paid pension plan and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Operates vehicle in a safe and environmentally responsible manner and maintains accurate and complete records pertaining to the transportation of dangerous goods

  • Operates the transport unit business to maintain and improve profitability by managing all aspects of operational costs

  • Assists in developing operating systems and procedures which reduce costs, maintain quality and ensure safe product delivery

  • Conducts daily pre and post-trip inspections in order to maintain the condition and safe operation of the truck and equipment

  • Verifies and confirms trucking documentation is completed in accordance with established company and regulatory requirements

  • Confirms product placement and volumes are correct according to documentation including load sheets, bills of lading and trip log

  • Completes all training and maintains appropriate certification and licensing as required by law for the transportation of dangerous goods

  • Adheres to and maintains all applicable company, legal and regulatory requirements

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in Leduc, AB or the surrounding area

  • Position working 60 hours per week.

  • No immigration sponsorship offered for this role

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School diploma, GED

  • Valid Class 1 driver's license

  • Acceptable Motor Vehicle Record (5 years)

  • 5+ years of oilfield or dangerous goods hauling experience

Physical Demand:

  • This is a safety-sensitive position, and the successful candidate will be required to complete drug and alcohol testing.
  • This role requires individual to be clean shaven to comply with customer site policy and safety requirement

  • Must be able to lift and carry a minimum of 50lbs

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Certification in WHMIS, TDG, First Aid and H2S

  • Experience operating forklifts, drum dollies, trailer hoists, chemical pumps, hoses, meters, printers and gauge

  • Must possess strong reading comprehension, writing and mathematical abilities

  • Must possess a strong safety focus and demonstrated history of safe practices

  • Experience handling products in various types of packages including bulk and drums

  • Ability to back straight trucks, semi-trucks and trailers into narrow passages through expensive industrial equipment

  • Experience with transportation of bulk liquids

  • Knowledge of equipment, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and Transport Canada regulations governing the transportation of hazardous materials

job.title: Plant Operator I
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has an immediate need for Plant Operator I located in Brunei. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.
 

What’s in it For You:

  • Career growth

  • Benefits and Rewards

What You will do:

  • Maintaining ChampionX’s warehouse facility to ensure proper handlings of chemicals, namely, receiving & dispatching

  • Ensure incoming materials are stored, stacked and distributed according to company procedures

  • Manage stock levels by participating in stock taking and cycle counting. Take inventory of raw and intermediate materials for ordering purposes

  • Issue materials to production preparations in accordance with operating procedures

  • Prepare finished goods for packing and dispatch.

  • Produce the highest standard of work possible in accordance with the company’s procedures

  • Prepare drums and tote tanks for loading and shipping which includes labelling of the container.

  • Operate all pumps for shipping and receiving of bulk materials and cleaning tote tank when needed

  • Operating of company forklifts & vehicles in day-to-day operations

  • Blending of chemicals according to company blend sheets.

  • Perform all required site inspections & housekeeping

  • Take part in safety meetings

  • Follow and promote continuous improvement of performance, safety and environmental policies and procedures.

  • Practice good housekeeping in all areas of the facility

  • Carry out any other function necessary for the smooth running of warehouse function

  • Performs miscellaneous job-related duties as assigned

Minimum Requirements*

  • Diploma and above.

  • More than 2 years of logistics related experience.

  • Proficient in operating forklifts, with forklift license.

  • Ability to maintain waste management program

  • Ability to foster effective working relationships within a team environment.

  • Knowledge and understanding of plant and warehouse operations and ability to maintain quality and safety standards.

  • Organizing and coordinating skills and ability to communicate effectively, both orally and in writing.

  • Knowledge of the Company product line, chemical composition, and likely disposal methods.

  • Knowledge of the structure and content of the English language

  • Forklift Operations Skills

  • Medium physical effort. Requires handling of average-weight objects up to 20 kilograms while standing & walking,

  • Work environment involves exposure to potentially dangerous materials and situations that require following extensive safety precautions and may include the use of protective equipment.

job.title: Chemist
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has a need for Chemist located in KL, Malaysia. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

As a Chemist at ChampionX, you will play a crucial role in the development, optimization and application of production chemical solutions to Oil and Gas customers. You will be responsible for running technical projects, conducting research, development, and coming up with technical solutions to address our customer’s challenges. You will work on projects both in the laboratory and in the field. As part of the role, you may be required to travel to different sites to conduct testing or meet with internal or external customers to discuss technical topics. The role will also allow the opportunity to present novel research or technical developments in conferences or industry events.

What You Will Do:
• Assist with Technical Support, Product Development and Selection.
• Conduct laboratory testing to develop, select or evaluate product performance, secondary properties and compatibilities. Prepare technical reports on the completed lab work.
• Support field-based projects. This may include troubleshooting of chemical related issues, conducting product selection and evaluation, and/or executing field trials.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams regionally and globally on technical projects, roll-out new developments and/or optimize internal processes.
• Implement and follow the company’s safety policy (Goal Zero) and procedures. Update laboratory test procedures and safety related documents where necessary.
• Support the maintenance of the day-to-day operations in the laboratory. Ensure equipment is calibrated and maintained.
• Take a professional approach to self-development. Identify appropriate training opportunities.
• Any other task that may reasonably be requested.

Desired Competencies:
• Readily learns and adopts new technologies
• Asks the right questions to analyse situations
• Takes on challenges of unfamiliar tasks and learns from experiences
 

Education and Experience:
Degree or Advanced degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering or related discipline.
Job Requirements:
• Primarily laboratory and office based
• Field work will be required
• Ability to travel overseas

Desirable Education and Experience:
• Previous experience of working with oilfield chemical related projects
• Previous experience of working in a laboratory
• Previous experience of managing day-to-day projects
• Good computer skills, must have experience in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint
• Good oral and written communication skills, speak and write English


Physical Requirements:
May require long periods of standing conducting experiments in a laboratory or travel to visit the field

About ChampionX:
ChampionX, provides specialty chemistry programs and related services for upstream and midstream oil and gas operations looking to maximize production, protect assets and reduce operating costs and environmental impact. Through on-site problem solving and innovative technologies, our team provides solutions that help energy customers overcome complex challenges in the world’s toughest energy frontiers.

job.title: Production Planner
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has future and immediate hiring needs for experienced Production Planner I professionals in Evansville, WY to support our operations team. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering competitive compensation and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, technology and continuous education
  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety to include top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment
  • Pride in working for a company that provides clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, matching 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Run MRP efficiently and schedule production to meet customer order due dates.
  • Develop and maintain daily, weekly, and monthly production plans to ensure customer service levels, minimize production costs, and optimize product sequencing.
  • Review the hot sheet daily, update plant remarks, and apply late codes as needed to drive key performance metrics such as SIFOT, Reliability, Responsiveness, and Overall Performance.
  • Participate in alignment meetings with critical cross-functional teams to ensure schedule accuracy and operational efficiency.
  • Provide daily status updates to internal and external stakeholders regarding open order exceptions, recommending re-planning actions to minimize non-conformances.
  • Identify and implement cost-reduction and availability improvement initiatives.
  • Review process orders to confirm raw material availability prior to releasing to production.
  • Schedule bulk orders to meet on-time loading requirements.
  • Manage batch adjustments, including cancellations, reschedules, and modifications as needed.
  • Enter repacks and relabels for customer-specific requirements.
  • Collaborate with the raw material planner to ensure timely material availability.
  • Lead scheduling for new product introductions and break-in batches.
  • Communicate daily with the production team on critical priorities, especially bulk loading needs.
  • Coordinate with tactical planning to prioritize schedules and meet customer commitments.
  • Work closely with the inventory team to resolve discrepancies and maintain accurate stock levels.
  • Adhere to all safety policies, supporting SLB’s Goal Zero principles to continually improve safety, health, environmental performance, and compliance.
  • Promote a culture of safety by reporting observations and incidents promptly.
  • Support and uphold the Quality Improvement Process, ensuring error-free work and a “do it right the first time” mindset.
  • Identify and drive process improvement opportunities within the planning function to support continuous improvement initiatives.
  • Resolves variances in inventory, conducts investigations and reports corrections of inventory to the Plant Manager.
  • Daily Review of all Process Orders for completeness and accuracy.
  • Generate Cycle Count Sheets, Freeze Inventory, and Create Inventory Documents in SAP.
  • Provides inventory SAP support for Materials Movement, Operations, and Administrative staff.
  • Provides daily, weekly, and monthly reporting as required.
  • Clear SAP System Database error logs by conducting investigations and makes appropriate corrections.
  • Process and schedule New Orders/Shortages
  • Other various tasks connected incoming materials and production scheduling

Position Details:

  • Ability to work in a fast-paced manufacturing and production environment
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and daily schedule changes
  • Role may require collaboration across production, inventory, and supply chain teams
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Supply Chain Management, Data Science, or a related subject
  • 2 years of relevant experience or an additional 4 years of experience in lieu of Bachelor’s degree
  • Knowledge of Scheduling & ERP systems, such as FST, and SAP
  • Working knowledge of Microsoft Excel
  • Good at managing workloads and flexible with daily changes
  • Understanding the complexities of the supply chain and how to maneuver through the challenges
  • Good written and oral communication skills
  • Experience with SAP, FST, and Warehouse Management
  • Background in Production Scheduling
  • Basic knowledge of plant operations

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience supporting production planning and scheduling activities in a manufacturing environment
  • Experience with inventory management and warehouse operations
  • Strong organizational and problem-solving skills
  • Prior experience that demonstrates strong ethics, effective time management, ability to multitask, adaptability, autonomy, and self-motivation
  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite, and ability to learn internal business systems)
  • Experience driving process improvements and operational efficiencies

Physical Demands:

  • May be required to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future
with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there. job.title: Water Treatment / Frac Specialist
job.description:
Job Posting Description

Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

job.title: Production Team Leader
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has immediate and future hiring needs for a Production Team Leader in Freeport, TX to support site operations. This is an opportunity to join a growing organization focused on operational excellence, safety, and continuous improvement, offering a competitive compensation package and comprehensive benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, technology, and continuous education
  • Thrive in a company that values a strong safety culture, including top-tier safety training and personal protective equipment
  • Pride in working for a company that supports clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments
  • Opportunity to lead teams and directly impact operational performance and continuous improvement
  • Comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, and 401(k) with company matching and more

What You Will Do:

  • Lead, train, and mentor a team of production associates to drive performance and engagement
  • Oversee day-to-day production activities to ensure safe, efficient, and high-quality operations
  • Provide skill-based instruction, coaching, and feedback while addressing personnel matters at the shift level
  • Ensure compliance with plant rules, policies, and safety standards; reinforce accountability across the team
  • Collaborate with the Technical Team to ensure all batches meet specifications, procedures, and documentation requirements
  • Clearly communicate shift objectives and align team performance with overall plant goals
  • Understand and communicate key performance metrics, ensuring team awareness and accountability
  • Monitor equipment performance, troubleshoot issues, and identify opportunities for efficiency improvements
  • Lead training and development progression for new and current employees
  • Partner with cross-functional teams (Quality, Planning, Engineering, Procurement, Logistics) to resolve production, service, and quality challenges
  • Support development of the site operations budget with a focus on continuous improvement of key performance metrics
  • Drive and promote Continuous Improvement initiatives to enhance safety, quality, delivery, and cost performance
  • Review procedures and documentation to ensure accuracy and adherence to SOPs and Good Documentation Practices
  • Coordinate operational improvement and redesign projects to meet performance targets and customer needs
  • Lead or participate in Root Cause Analysis to resolve safety, quality, and service issues
  • Perform additional duties as assigned

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s Degree OR High School Diploma / GED with 4 years of experience in manufacturing, logistics, or related environment
  • 2 years of supervisory experience in a manufacturing or logistics environment
  • Strong leadership and team development experience
  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Engineering Degree, Process Technology Degree, or equivalent
  • Experience with SAP, MS Access, and Microsoft Excel
  • Exposure to Continuous Improvement and/or Lean Manufacturing principles
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to exercise independent judgment, think critically, and collaborate across cross-functional teams
  • Proven ability to drive accountability, improve processes, and lead change initiatives

Physical Demands:

  • May be required to lift, push, pull, or carry materials as needed in a plant environment
  • Ability to work in a manufacturing setting, including standing, walking, and interacting with equipment
  • Role may be subject to safety requirements and site-specific protocols

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We unlock access to energy for the benefit of all while advancing a future with more energy and less carbon. Our diverse and forward-thinking teams are committed to developing the technologies needed to power the future responsibly.

For more information, visit SLB.com

job.title: Regional Sales Manager - South Texas Region
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX (SLB) has an immediate need for a Regional Sales Manager - South Texas Region. This person will need to be within located a 60 mile radius of Kennedy, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement.

  • The chance to join a learning and problem-solving culture

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

Sales Strategy and Planning

  • Effectively develop and execute strategic regional sales plans for chemical injection pumps within the South Texas Region, with focus on the Kennedy, Texas territory
  • Evaluate and implement appropriate sales techniques to support the strategic growth objectives while maintaining a profitable gross margin & repeatable sales revenues
  • Manage sales, profit margins and operational expenses for designated sales territory
  • Develop and update reliable sales forecasts
  • Identify, qualify, and convert project and new customer sales opportunities that will contribute to the territory revenues
  • Develop install base in adjacent segments to diversify market share in other regions outside of O&G
  • Provide periodic sales activity report

Customer Relationship Management

  • Set-up customer appointments to conduct sales presentations to promote, sell, and apply our complete product line in a regional sales area
  • Manage assigned accounts as well as developing new accounts, projects, or other sales opportunities
  • Provide product training at the field and corporate levels for all customer types
  • Provide customers with technical support and expertise in reviewing specifications and applying the products
  • Attend relevant product conventions, sales seminars, and tradeshows

Market Analysis

  • Develop annual marketing strategy in conjunction with the Director of Sales and the inside sales team
  • Identify joint marketing opportunities where applicable
  • Perform market assessments, competitor analyses, pricing strategies and execute counter strategies to gain market share
  • Draw conclusions from market assessment data on the competitive environment and the company’s strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and targets

Collaboration

  • Develop relationships with internal sales teams, leverage synergies and drive joint sales opportunities
  • Collaborate and provide performance feedback to Manufacture Representatives/Distributors for the area of responsibility
  • Communicate with the Sales Team and the Production Team regularly to guarantee that sales and client objectives are met

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in San Antonio, TX or surrounding areas
  • Route territory will be the South Texas Region
  • Compensation package offered: Competitive base salary with eligibility for a quarterly sales incentive bonus plan
  • New hire product training, new hire onboarding and orientation provided
  • Plant visits will consist of office and cubicle environments in a manufacturing facility with PPE requirements
  • Immigration is not offered for this position

Minimum Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering, business, or equivalent experience.
  • 3 – 5 years of experience in customer-facing, outside sales of oil and gas
  • Willingness to travel up to 50-75% in the specified sales territory
  • Contract negotiation experience
  • Experience creating and presenting complex sales reports

Soft Skills:

  • Adapt at managing multiple tasks simultaneously
  • Results Oriented - Good follow up skills to ensure strategy implementation
  • Provide leadership through example and adequate direction
  • Able to communicate with customers at all levels
  • Able to build relationships with company and customer contacts
  • Professional and positive attitude
  • Passion for continuous improvement
  • Open to change and new information
  • Promote collaboration and share best practices towards the larger strategic business goals.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience working with chemical injection pumps
  • Experience with Automation
  • Technical expertise in oil and gas chemical injections pumps
  • Knowledge of Wellmark & Timberline products

​Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.
  • Minimal climbing stairs, lifting between 10lbs up to 25lbs, handling, grasping, turning, holding or using hands, dialing, standing, walking, hearing and writing
  • Occasional speaking between 11% and 33%
  • Constant use of a keyboard, mouse and viewing a computer monitor, sitting, and seeing greater than 66%

job.title: EOR Chemist
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are seeking a Chemist for our Chemically Enhanced Oil Recovery (CEOR) Technology team located in Aberdeen, UK to support both business and customer needs.  The Chemist will be responsible for supporting more senior team members with lab testing, managing basic customer support projects, and supporting a safe and efficient laboratory.  The ideal candidate is versatile, communicates effectively, and thrives in a collaborative environment. If you are a passionate professional that likes to work in an autonomous, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that’s passionate about growth.
  • The support of an organization that believes it’s vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.
  • The opportunity to thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture, and empowers its employees through continuous improvement.
  • Flexible working arrangements and a comprehensive benefits package starting on day one of employment, including medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Apply basic chemistry principles, theories and concepts to assignments where precedents typically exist
  • Use basic scientific knowledge to solve problems
  • Contribute to the research and development of differentiated technology, products, processes and systems.
  • Perform work in a safe manner while identifying opportunities for improving safety
  • Manage several requests at once while working within a fast-paced environment

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor of Science in Chemistry, Physics, Biology, Biochemistry, or related field
  • Immigration Sponsorship is not available for this role.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience working with polymers and/or surfactants
  • Experience using a viscometer/rheometer, preparing samples in a glove box, using a coreflood, and running contact angle/IFT measurements
  • Excellent written and oral communication skills and the ability to communicate within all levels of the organization
  • The candidate should be outgoing, creative, a self-starter, and team oriented

About ChampionX

ChampionX is a global leader in providing chemistry programs and services, drilling technology, artificial lift solutions, and automation technologies for the upstream and midstream oil and gas industry. Our world-class safety culture fuels our purpose to improve lives through our commitment to deliver sustainable operations.
